input.text: A safe room, as defined by FEMA, is designed to provide shelter from tornadoes. However, if the safe room is located below ground, it may not be suitable for protection during a weapons of mass destruction event. The Red Cross recommends safe rooms for areas prone to tornadoes. It's important not to confuse safe rooms for tornadoes with shelter-in-place rooms. An above-ground safe room with no windows can also be used as a shelter-in-place location. It's important to note that a safe room used for protection from windstorms is different from a room selected for shelter-in-place, although they serve a similar purpose. If an above-ground safe room for windstorms has no windows, it can also be an ideal location for sheltering-in-place.

input.speaker: agent
input.text: Each disaster has its characteristics, but it's important to plan and prepare today for you and your family to recover from an emergency tomorrow. Here are some steps you can take: 1. Get an Emergency Supplies kit lasting up to three days per family member, and remember supplies for your pets. 2. Make a family communication plan, including shelter-in-place and evacuation procedures. 3. Stay informed by visiting Ready.gov and signing up for emergency notifications from San Mateo County (SMC) Alert. 4. Get involved by signing up for a Community Emergency Response Team (CERT) course and learning life-saving techniques in a CPR class. Remember, earthquakes can happen suddenly and without warning. Identifying potential hazards and planning can reduce the risk of severe injury or loss of life during an earthquake. If your home is damaged, check for gas leaks and avoid using toilets if you suspect sewage lines are damaged. Contact the gas company and a plumber if needed. In the same way, if water pipes are damaged, please contact the water company and avoid using tap water. You can obtain safe water by melting ice cubes. Each county prone to earthquakes has guides to help citizens before, during, and after an earthquake. Please reach out to your city hall for more information.

input.text: Although I don't have the exact information about which state has more wildfires, it is known that the 2018 wildfire season in California was the deadliest and most destructive on record in the state. In the 2020 California wildfire season, record-setting wildfires burned across the state, with nearly 10,000 fires burning over 4.2 million acres by the end of the year. This accounted for more than 4% of the state's roughly 100 million acres of land, making 2020 the largest wildfire season in California's modern history. The August Complex fire, part of the 2020 wildfire year, has been described as the first "gigafire" due to the area burned exceeding 1 million acres. This fire spread across seven counties and was larger than the state of Rhode Island.

input.text: Is flooding the most common type of disaster?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: Yes, floods are the most common disasters in the United States and can result from various sources such as rain, snow, coastal storms, and dam overflows. They can develop slowly or quickly, with flash floods occurring suddenly and without warning. Floods can cause power outages, disrupt transportation, damage buildings, and lead to landslides. Their impact can range from local to widespread, affecting entire river basins and multiple states. While some floods develop slowly over days, flash floods can emerge within minutes, carrying dangerous debris and swiftly sweeping away whatever is in their path.

input.text: What is ground water contamination?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: Groundwater contamination refers to the presence of harmful substances, such as household cleaners, flea powders, garden pesticides, paints, varnishes, motor oil, and antifreeze. It occurs when these products are disposed of improperly and seep into the ground.  By disposing of these harmful products improperly, we are contributing to groundwater contamination.

input.speaker: user
input.text: Can it be clean up?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: Yes, contaminated groundwater can be cleaned up, but it can be a time-consuming and expensive process. Communities whose drinking water source has become contaminated have had to spend millions of dollars to remove contaminants from the water before it can be piped to homes and businesses. The cleanup is effective, but it can significantly affect the cost of providing the water to customers. It is far better to prevent contamination in the first place.

Communities whose drinking water source has become contaminated have had to spend millions of dollars to remove contaminants from the water before it can be piped to homes and businesses. The cleanup is effective, but it can significantly affect the cost of providing the water to customers. It is far better to prevent contamination in the first place.

input.text: Contrary to popular belief, groundwater is not found in underground rivers or lakes. input.speaker: user
input.text: What are the suppliers for the city?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: The City of Monterey Park Water System gets its water from local groundwater, which is pumped from 12 City-owned wells. These wells have a total capacity of 20 million gallons per day (mgd) and reach a depth of up to 1,800 feet. They are located near the Rio Hondo, outside the city limits, in the Main San Gabriel groundwater basin. Monterey Park's water is supplied by the San Gabriel Valley Municipal Water District, of which the city is a member. It's important to note that the City of Monterey Park does not own the Garvey Reservoir, and the water it contains is not stored for city use.

input.text: What's the difference between Market Cap and NAV?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: The market cap is simply the share price multiplied by the number of shares. It doesn't indicate any inherent value; rather, it reflects what people believe the company is worth. NAV, or net asset value, is the total value of the company's assets divided by the number of shares. This is also referred to as the book value. In summary, market cap is calculated by multiplying the share price by the number of shares.

input.text: Enterprise value is the estimated cost of acquiring a company. Typically, companies are acquired without any debt or cash, so any cash on the balance sheet at the time of the transaction is given to the original owners. The Enterprise Value represents the fundamental total value of the firm. It excludes cash because paying for cash with cash doesn't make sense. This also simplifies the analysis of transaction multiples, as some companies might have a significant amount of extra cash on their balance sheet, which could distort the multiples.

input.speaker: user
input.text: How is it calculated?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: Calculating a company's enterprise value requires considering several factors. The equation for enterprise value is as follows: EV = Market Cap + Minority Interest + Preferred Stock + Debt—Cash. Enterprise value represents the total value of a company to all its shareholders, including creditors, preferred stockholders, and common stockholders. It's important to note that taking on debt can either increase or decrease the enterprise value, depending on the company's cash balance.

input.speaker: user
input.text: should I set up my second bank account with different bank I have right now?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: There are advantages and disadvantages to keeping all your accounts with the same bank versus having accounts at different banks. If you open a second account at a different bank, you may have more options for products like Mortgage or credit extensions since you are not limited to just one institution. If you use the same bank, that would make it harder to lose track of, and you could probably still get reports summarizing the balances across them. Additionally, occasionally there are also perks like the bank spontaneously offering you increased credit or even a whole line of credit because the bank has your data already.

input.speaker: user
input.text: what are people's experience with using two bank accounts?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: Some people separate off regular planned expenses (rent/mortgage, utility bills, insurance premiums) from spending money. For example, one gets a direct deposit totaling the sum of regular monthly bills and a prorated provision for longer term regular bills like semi-annual car insurance premiums; and the other one gets a direct deposit of the rest which I then allocate to savings and spending. For another example, use account B for all my expenses (rent, credit card, food, etc) and use account A to save money.

input.text: can you let me know advantages of using two bank accounts?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: You will be able to hold different currencies if you have two bank accounts. Banks in certain countries are offering what is called a dual currency account. It is essentially 2 accounts with same account number but different currency.

input.speaker: user
input.text: How to pay with cash when car shopping?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: When paying with cash for a car, you need to get a cashier's check made out for the amount. Note that dealers may not accept personal checks as they may bounce. You would typically pay a deposit of $500, which can be a check or bank deposit after negotiation. Then, you can return to the dealership later the same day or within a few days with a cashier's check for the remaining amount. Keep in mind that if you give the dealership $10,000 or more in actual cash, they will be required to fill out a form 8300 with the IRS.

input.text: Do I really need an extra warranty on my new car?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: An insurance via "extended warranty" is rarely a good deal.  Don't waste money on a third party warranty.  A new vehicle does not have unknown risk associated with the purchase, and also comes with peace of mind through a manufacturer warranty.  It is also depending on various factors, such as your driving habits and your risk tolerance.  If you drive less, have a good track record with cars, and are comfortable with the manufacturer's warranty, you might not need an additional warranty. However, if you drive more, have had issues with cars in the past, or want additional peace of mind, purchasing an extended warranty could be beneficial.

input.speaker: agent
input.text: The longevity of EV batteries can indeed impact the used car market. The companies like Toyota and GM have done extensive testing and research to ensure the longevity of their battery packs. For instance, Toyota's battery packs have proven to last longer than initially claimed, debunking the notion that they would need replacement every five years. Similarly, GM has made similar claims about the longevity of their packs. However, Nissan's decision to omit a liquid cooling system in their cars' battery packs has led to heat-related issues, affecting their longevity.  Enhanced battery tech investment needed badly, but not anytime soon while batteries are not the only bottleneck in vehicle production.

input.text: What is the goal of NASA‚Äôs Europa Clipper mission?

input.speaker: user
input.text: What is the goal of NASA‚Äôs Europa Clipper mission?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: The main science goal of NASA's Europa Clipper mission is to determine whether there are places below the surface of Jupiter's icy moon, Europa, that could support life. The mission's three main science objectives are to understand the nature of the ice shell and the ocean beneath it, to investigate its composition, and to characterize its geology. The mission’s detailed exploration of Europa will help scientists better understand the astrobiological potential for habitable worlds beyond our planet.

input.speaker: user
input.text: I Europa livable for humans?

input.speaker: agent
input.text:
Europa is not considered directly habitable for humans, but it is one of the most promising environments in our solar system due to the presence of a vast ocean beneath its icy crust. This ocean could potentially contain conditions suitable for life as we know it. The Europa Clipper mission is designed to investigate whether the moon could have conditions suitable for life, but it is not a life detection mission. Europa could have all the necessary "ingredients" for life as we know it: 
- Water: Twice as much as Earth’s oceans
- Organics: Essential chemical building blocks from a variety of sources
- Energy: Chemical energy sources from the surface and the sea floor
- Stability: Conditions remaining similar for 4 billion years

input.speaker: user
input.text: The surface of Jupiter Europa

input.speaker: agent
input.text: The surface of Jupiter's moon, Europa, is made up of ice. It is covered in a thick crust of frozen water that is estimated to be between 10 to 100 kilometers thick. The surface also shows signs of past and present geological activity, such as ridges, chaos terrain, and lenticulae. These features suggest that there is heat being generated from within Europa, which is thought to be due to tidal heating caused by Jupiter's gravitational pull. Additionally, the surface of Europa is subject to bombardment by radiation from Jupiter, which can cause the ice to darken and form a reddish-brown color in some areas. The surface of Jupiter's moon Europa features a widely varied landscape, including ridges, bands, small rounded domes and disrupted spaces that geologists call "chaos terrain."

These features suggest that there is heat being generated from within Europa, which is thought to be due to tidal heating caused by Jupiter's gravitational pull. Additionally, the surface of Europa is subject to bombardment by radiation from Jupiter, which can cause the ice to darken and form a reddish-brown color in some areas. The surface of Jupiter's moon Europa features a widely varied landscape, including ridges, bands, small rounded domes and disrupted spaces that geologists call "chaos terrain."

input.text: What is the goal of NASA‚Äôs Europa Clipper mission?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: The main science goal of NASA's Europa Clipper mission is to determine whether there are places below the surface of Jupiter's icy moon, Europa, that could support life. The mission's three main science objectives are to understand the nature of the ice shell and the ocean beneath it, to investigate its composition, and to characterize its geology. The mission’s detailed exploration of Europa will help scientists better understand the astrobiological potential for habitable worlds beyond our planet.

input.speaker: user
input.text: I Europa livable for humans?

input.speaker: agent
input.text:
Europa is not considered directly habitable for humans, but it is one of the most promising environments in our solar system due to the presence of a vast ocean beneath its icy crust. This ocean could potentially contain conditions suitable for life as we know it. The Europa Clipper mission is designed to investigate whether the moon could have conditions suitable for life, but it is not a life detection mission. Europa could have all the necessary "ingredients" for life as we know it: 
- Water: Twice as much as Earth’s oceans
- Organics: Essential chemical building blocks from a variety of sources
- Energy: Chemical energy sources from the surface and the sea floor
- Stability: Conditions remaining similar for 4 billion years

input.text: The propulsion subsystem of the Europa Clipper will carry fuel and oxidizer to change its velocity by about 3,600 miles per hour (5,800 kilometers per hour). While this is a significant and crucial capability, it represents only about five percent of the velocity change needed for the mission. The propulsion subsystem can be thought of as the "gas tank" of the Europa Clipper.

input.text: Yes, IBM offers [IBM Cloudant](https://cloud.ibm.com/docs/Cloudant?topic=Cloudant-documents) which stores JSON objects.

input.speaker: user
input.text: So it can store any random JSON object or I need to specify fields in advances?

input.speaker: agent
input.text:
Yes, IBM Cloudant can store any JSON object subject to some caveats listed below
1. All documents must have two fields:
    * A unique _id field. The _id field is detailed in the next section.
    * A _rev field. The _rev field is a revision identifier, and is essential to the IBM Cloudant replication protocol.
2. Field names that begin with the underscore character (_) are reserved in IBM Cloudant. This rule means that you can't normally have your own field names that begin with an underscore. For example, the field example would be accepted, but the field _example would result in a doc_validation error message.

input.speaker: user
input.text: What if I want to store an image or pdf with a document?

input.speaker: agent
input.text:
IBM Cloudant has support for storing attachments alongside documents, a long-standing feature it inherits from CouchDB. So you can easily add an image or PDF as an attachment to your document but you must consider a few things before you use attachments in IBM Cloudant today, especially if you’re looking at larger assets.

1. IBM Cloudant is expensive as a block store.
2. IBM Cloudant’s internal implementation is not efficient in handling large amounts of binary data. So, slow and expensive. IBM Cloudant is acceptable for small assets and occasional use. As a rule, if you need to store binary data alongside IBM Cloudant documents, it’s better to use a separate solution more suited for this purpose. You need store only the attachment metadata in the IBM Cloudant document.

input.text:
It's quite easy to get setup with IBM Cloudant instance. Let me walk you through it.

1. Log in to your IBM Cloud account, and click Create resource. 

2. Type Cloudant in the Search bar and click to open it. 

3. Select an offering and an environment. 

4. Type an instance name. 

5. Select your plan. 

6. To create the service, click Create:
After you click Create, the system displays a message to say that the instance is being provisioned, which returns you to the Resource list. From the Resource list, you see that the status for your instance is, Provision in progress. 

7. After you create an instance, the status changes to Active.

input.speaker: user
input.text: What are difference between lite and standard plan?

input.speaker: agent
input.text:
The Lite plan is free but has a fixed amount of throughput capacity. The paid Standard plan offers configurable provisioned throughput capacity and data storage pricing that scales as your application requirements change.  

Here are the couple of specific differences
- In the case of a Lite plan,  provisioned throughput capacity is fixed at 20 reads per second, 10 writes per second, and 5 global queries per second, and data storage is capped at 1 GB. A Standard plan starts with a provisioned throughput capacity of 100 reads per second, 50 writes per second, and 5 global queries per second with 20 GB of data storage with an easy way to increase them in small increments.  
- You're limited to one IBM Cloudant Lite plan instance per IBM Cloud account.

input.speaker: user
input.text: So does that mean if I am just exploring things for now, I should stick to to Lite plan?

input.text: What approaches are there for pricing a small business?

input.speaker: user
input.text: What approaches are there for pricing a small business?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: One approach is to determine the lowest amount a seller would accept, which is the liquidation value. This involves calculating the value of the business's assets, such as land, house, furnishings, and accounts payable, minus any liabilities. Another approach is to price the business based on a multiple of its annual earnings. This can be done using a discounted cash flow analysis, where you project the business's earnings out in perpetuity and discount it by the going interest rate. Alternatively, you can use an industry multiplier to multiply the business's EBITDA (Earnings Before Interest, Taxes, Depreciation, and Amortization) by a factor common in the industry. From the documents, it appears that the 2011 EBITDA multiplier for hospitality industries is 13.8. and in the end for small business in most sectors you can use a rule of thumb, 2x net + FFE.

input.text: What could be the reason that most small business fail along the way?

input.text: What does it makes a business to go forward?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: A business can move forward successfully by having a solid business model with minimal startup costs and low overhead costs. It's important to have a competitive advantage and offer extra value to customers. This can be achieved by becoming savvy in spotting good deals and reselling products for a higher price. Additionally you need to be careful with the structure, do all the research; understand the risk and opportunities as much you can.  Indeed to move forward in your business you need to make the things happen, you have to do the marketing and sale-link up with your prospective customers. And you have to have a well designed and well operating business. The key to good business management is ""do unto others as you would have them do unto you"" - ie. look at your business from your customers' perspective. In the end the point of a business is to make a profit . However, have some criteria for success or failure. Don't end up caught in the sunk costs fallacy. If the business isn't going to provide a better life than working, pull the plug early.

input.speaker: user
input.text: What approaches are there for pricing a small business?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: One approach is to determine the lowest amount a seller would accept, which is the liquidation value. This involves calculating the value of the business's assets, such as land, house, furnishings, and accounts payable, minus any liabilities. Another approach is to price the business based on a multiple of its annual earnings. This can be done using a discounted cash flow analysis, where you project the business's earnings out in perpetuity and discount it by the going interest rate. Alternatively, you can use an industry multiplier to multiply the business's EBITDA (Earnings Before Interest, Taxes, Depreciation, and Amortization) by a factor common in the industry. From the documents, it appears that the 2011 EBITDA multiplier for hospitality industries is 13.8. and in the end for small business in most sectors you can use a rule of thumb, 2x net + FFE.

input.speaker: user
input.text: How to read bond yield quotes? What do the time, coupon, price, yield, and time mean?

input.speaker: user
input.text: How to read bond yield quotes? What do the time, coupon, price, yield, and time mean?

input.speaker: agent
input.text:
Bond yield quotes provide important information about a bond's yield, which is the annualized return on investment for the bond. Here's how to read bond yield quotes:
* Time: This refers to the time of the quote presented and indicates when the quote was generated.
* Coupon (coupon rate): This is the amount of interest paid on the bond each period, as specified on the coupon itself. It is usually expressed as a percentage of the bond's face value.
* Price: This is the normalised price of the bond, representing the price of taking a position of $100 worth of the principal in the bond. It is often expressed as a percentage of the bond's face value.
* Yield: This is the interest rate that you would receive by buying at the current price. It is the inverse of the price and represents the annualized return on investment for the bond.
To summarize, when reading bond yield quotes, the time refers to the quote's generation time, while the coupon, price, and yield provide information about the bond's interest payments, market value, and annualized return on investment, respectively.

input.speaker: user
input.text: Whata is the safest type of bond to invest in?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: The safest place to put money is a mixture of cash, local municipal bond funds with average durations under two years and US Treasury bond funds with short duration. Among government bonds, U.S. Treasury securities are often regarded as the safest, followed by municipal bonds and government agency bonds. However, it is essential to note that even the safest bonds come with some level of risk, and do not make much money.  If you do choose to invest in bonds, some bonds being riskier than others,  I recommend that you diversify your risks by investing in a bond fund, rather than in just one company's or government's debt. This will reduce the likelihood that you will experience a catastrophic loss. investing in a short-term, high quality bond fund or a tax-free bond fund with short durations is a good way to balance safety vs. return. Additionally, You should talk to a financial fiduciary (make sure they are a fiduciary, not all planners are) about investing your money.

input.speaker: user
input.text: Investing in foreign bonds

input.speaker: agent
input.text: Adding international bonds to an individual investor's portfolio is a controversial subject. On top of the standard risks of bonds, you are adding country specific risk, currency risk and diversifying your individual company risk. In theory many of these risks should be rewarded but the data are noisy at best and adding risk like developed currency risk may not be rewarded at all.  However, if your home currency (or currency union) does not have a deep bond market and/or bonds make up most of your portfolio, it is probably worth diversifying a chunk of your bond exposure internationally.

input.speaker: user
input.text: What are the grounds for lodging an appeal?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: In most cases, the appellate court considers two main points: whether a legal mistake was made in the trial court and whether this mistake affected the final decision (referred to as the "judgment"). The appellate court only reviews what happened in the trial court to determine if a legal mistake occurred in the original trial. An appeal is a process through which a case is moved from a lower court to a higher court for review or reconsideration after the lower court has issued a judgment. The higher or appellate court may uphold, reverse, or modify the decision.

input.speaker: user
input.text: Who is responsible for covering my expenses?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: The Court of Appeal's judgment usually includes costs on appeal. In most instances, if a party prevails on appeal, they can request the other party to cover their court costs. In some cases, the winning party may also request the other party to cover their attorney fees. - After the court issues a remittitur (the final step in an appeal), the prevailing party can file a memorandum of costs in the trial court. The trial court will handle the request and notify the parties about the next steps. - If the parties have questions regarding reimbursement of appeal costs, they can contact the trial court. There are fees in both the trial court and the Court of Appeal during an appeal. If you wish to have your fees waived in both courts, you must obtain a fee waiver.

input.speaker: user
input.text: What are the grounds for lodging an appeal?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: In most cases, the appellate court considers two main points: whether a legal mistake was made in the trial court and whether this mistake affected the final decision (referred to as the "judgment"). The appellate court only reviews what happened in the trial court to determine if a legal mistake occurred in the original trial. An appeal is a process through which a case is moved from a lower court to a higher court for review or reconsideration after the lower court has issued a judgment. The higher or appellate court may uphold, reverse, or modify the decision.

input.speaker: user
input.text: Who is responsible for covering my expenses?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: The Court of Appeal's judgment usually includes costs on appeal. In most instances, if a party prevails on appeal, they can request the other party to cover their court costs. In some cases, the winning party may also request the other party to cover their attorney fees. - After the court issues a remittitur (the final step in an appeal), the prevailing party can file a memorandum of costs in the trial court. The trial court will handle the request and notify the parties about the next steps. - If the parties have questions regarding reimbursement of appeal costs, they can contact the trial court. There are fees in both the trial court and the Court of Appeal during an appeal. If you wish to have your fees waived in both courts, you must obtain a fee waiver.

input.text: The documents do not specify jail sentences, but you can opt for the Division of Child Support Services (DCSS) to enforce your support orders. The Office of Child Support Enforcement (OCSE) is the federal agency responsible for administering the nationwide child support program. Offset refers to the money taken from a parent's state or federal income tax refund or an administrative payment, like federal retirement benefits, to pay a child support debt.

input.text: When you enable web chat security, all messages received by the web chat integration that are not accompanied by a properly signed JSON Web Token (JWT) will be rejected. input.text: what makes the different shapes of the moon

input.speaker: agent
input.text: The different shapes of the Moon, also known as lunar phases, are determined by the amount of sunlight that is illuminating the portion of the Moon visible from Earth. This changes as the Moon orbits the Earth and the relative positions of the Moon, Earth, and Sun shift. When the Sun, Earth, and Moon are aligned, with the Sun and Moon on opposite sides of the Earth, we see a full moon. When the Moon is positioned between the Earth and the Sun, we see a new moon. The crescent and gibbous shapes occur during the intervals between these principal phases, when only a portion of the Moon's surface is illuminated. The specific shape at any given time depends on the Moon's position in its orbit. When a sphere is illuminated on one hemisphere and viewed from a different angle , the portion of the illuminated area that is visible will have a two - dimensional shape defined by the intersection of an ellipse and circle ( where the major axis of the ellipse coincides with a diameter of the circle ) . If the half - ellipse is convex with respect to the half - circle , then the shape will be gibbous ( bulging outwards ) whereas if the half - ellipse is concave with respect to the half - circle , then the shape will be a crescent .

input.text: The different shapes of the Moon, also known as lunar phases, are determined by the amount of sunlight that is illuminating the portion of the Moon visible from Earth. This changes as the Moon orbits the Earth and the relative positions of the Moon, Earth, and Sun shift. When the Sun, Earth, and Moon are aligned, with the Sun and Moon on opposite sides of the Earth, we see a full moon. When the Moon is positioned between the Earth and the Sun, we see a new moon. The crescent and gibbous shapes occur during the intervals between these principal phases, when only a portion of the Moon's surface is illuminated. The specific shape at any given time depends on the Moon's position in its orbit. When a sphere is illuminated on one hemisphere and viewed from a different angle , the portion of the illuminated area that is visible will have a two - dimensional shape defined by the intersection of an ellipse and circle ( where the major axis of the ellipse coincides with a diameter of the circle ) . If the half - ellipse is convex with respect to the half - circle , then the shape will be gibbous ( bulging outwards ) whereas if the half - ellipse is concave with respect to the half - circle , then the shape will be a crescent .

input.speaker: user
input.text: What is the distance between the moon and earth?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: The average distance from the Moon to the Earth is about 238,856 miles or 384,402 kilometers. But this distance varies throughout the Moon's orbit, ranging from about 221,500 miles or 385,000 kilometers at its closest, to about 252,700 miles or 406,700 kilometers at its farthest resulting in a differential range of 50,200 kilometers or 31,200 miles. Lunar distance (astronomy) Astronomers O'Keefe and Anderson calculated the lunar distance by observing 4 occultations from 9 locations in 1952 . They calculated a mean distance of 7008384407600000000 ♠ 384 407. 6 ± 4.7 km , however the value was refined by in 1962 by Irene Fischer , who incorporated updated geodetic data to produce a value of 7008384403700000000 ♠ 384 403. 7 ± 2 km .

input.text: Vallabhbhai Patel, popularly known as Sardar Patel , was the first Deputy Prime Minister of India . He acted as de facto Supreme Commander - in - chief of Indian army during the political integration of India and the Indo - Pakistani War of 1947 . He is credited by historians with ensuring the success of the rebellion across India. He was arrested and was imprisoned with the entire Congress Working Committee from 1942 to 1945

input.text: A great starting point would be to buy and read the book 'The Intelligent Investor' by Benjamin Graham. Another great way is to join an investment club. They club will act like a mutual fund, investing in stocks researched and selected by the group.

input.speaker: user
input.text: Is it safer to invest in bonds?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: Investing in bonds can generally be considered safer than investing in stocks, as the issuer would need to default (usually this means they are in bankruptcy) in order for you not to be paid back. For example, German bonds have been considered safer than Greek bonds recently based on the underlying strength of the government. Unlike Cash Equivalents, these are not guaranteed against loss, which means that if the issuer defaults, you could lose up to 100% of your investment. In addition, if you need to sell the bond before it matures, you could lose money.  If you do choose to invest in bonds, diversify your risks by investing in a bond fund, rather than in just one company's or government's debt. Debt is considered a safer investment than equity for any particular company, government, or the market as a whole.

input.speaker: agent
input.text: The tax implications of selling stocks for a profit depend on various factors, including the length of time you held the stocks, your income level, and location. In some countries, income received from dividends is taxed as income, whereas profits on share trades are capital gains.  If you sell your shares for more than their value at the time you received them then you will be liable for capital gains tax - but only if the profit exceeds your annual allowance.

input.speaker: user
input.text: Explain the term financial fiduciary

input.speaker: agent
input.text: A financial fiduciary is a person or organization that has a legal obligation to act in the best interest of another party when it comes to managing their financial affairs. This means that the fiduciary must put the client's interests above their own and avoid any conflicts of interest. Between that requirement and the fact that they are paid for their time (and not on commission), they have zero incentive to try to sell you anything that you do not need. Their only job is to help you with your financial situation.

input.text: where was the cotton gin invented and where is it used today

input.speaker: user
input.text: where was the cotton gin invented and where is it used today

input.speaker: agent
input.text: According to the worm gear roller gin, it was invented in the Indian subcontinent, specifically during the early Delhi Sultanate era of the 13th-14th centuries. It came into use in the Mughal Empire around the 16th century and is still used in the Indian subcontinent today. However, it's important to note that there were different types of cotton gins, including a handheld roller gin used in India since at least 500 CE, and a single-roller cotton gin found in the Ajanta caves of India and used until innovations were made in the form of foot-powered gins. The modern mechanical cotton gin was created by American inventor Eli Whitney in 1793 and patented in 1794, but I have no information as to its current usage. The early history of the cotton gin is ambiguous , because archeologists likely mistook the cotton gin 's parts for other tools .

input.text: Did the need for cotton workers increase?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: Yes, the demand for cotton workers did increase after the invention of the modern mechanical cotton gin by Eli Whitney in 1793. This invention revolutionized the cotton industry in the United States, leading to a rapid increase in the demand for cotton workers. This increased demand for labor has been identified as a contributing factor to the growth of slavery in the American South.  The number of slaves rose in concert with the increase in cotton production , increasing from around 700,000 in 1790 to around 3.2 million in 1850.

input.text: Was the invention of the cotton gin indirectly a cause of the American Civil War?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: Yes, the invention of the cotton gin has been identified as an inadvertent contributing factor to the outbreak of the American Civil War. The increased demand for cotton workers, primarily filled by slaves, in the American South after the invention of the cotton gin led to the growth of slavery and tensions between the North and South over the issue of slavery. These tensions were one of the main causes of the American Civil War.  Because of its inadvertent effect on American slavery , and on its ensuring that the South 's economy developed in the direction of plantation - based agriculture , the invention of the cotton gin is frequently cited as one of the indirect causes of the American Civil War .

input.speaker: user
input.text: Modern cotton gins.

input.speaker: agent
input.text: Modern cotton gins have evolved from the early mechanical cotton gins. They use multiple powered cleaning cylinders and saws to automate the process and offer far higher productivity than their hand-powered forebears. These modern gins are used worldwide, including in the United States and the Indian subcontinent, to efficiently process large quantities of cotton fiber. The cotton enters a dryer , which removes excess moisture. The cylinder cleaner uses six or seven rotating , spiked cylinders to break up large clumps of cotton . Finer foreign material , such as soil and leaves , passes through rods or screens for removal . The stick machine uses centrifugal force to remove larger foreign matter , such as sticks and burrs , while the cotton is held by rapidly rotating saw cylinders .

input.text: What are the steps to be taken to gather the relevant worker node data?

input.speaker: user
input.text: What are the steps to be taken to gather the relevant worker node data?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: There are general steps to gather the relevant worker node data: First,  Check the conditions of your worker nodes and cluster before you gather data. This includes checking the CPU and memory level of your nodes. If any node is over 80% in either CPU or memory usage, consider provisioning more nodes or reducing your workload.Secondly if all worker nodes in a cluster, or in a single zone, subnet, or VLAN are affected, you can open an initial support ticket without gathering data. However, you might later be asked to gather the relevant data. If only one or some of your worker nodes are affected, you must gather the relevant data to include in your support ticket.  Before opening a support ticket, it is recommended to review the information and follow any troubleshooting steps in Debugging worker nodes, Worker node states, and Troubleshooting worker nodes in Critical or NotReady state. To gather worker node data, you can use the command 'kubectl top node' (for Kubernetes clusters) or 'oc top node' (for OpenShift clusters), which will display the CPU and memory usage of each node and once you have gathered this information, include it in your support ticket when you open one.

input.speaker: user
input.text: How can i update a classic worker node?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: An update is available for your worker nodes in a classic infrastructure cluster. As security updates and patches are put in place for the API server and other master components, you must be sure that the worker nodes remain in sync. You can make two types of updates: updating only the patch version, or updating the major.minor version with the patch version. For Classic clusters with a storage solution such as OpenShift Data Foundation you must cordon, drain, and replace each worker node sequentially. If you deployed OpenShift Data Foundation to a subset of worker nodes in your cluster, then after you replace the worker node, you must then edit the ocscluster resource to include the new worker node.You can update the master Kubernetes version with the ibmcloud ks cluster master updatecommand. Remember that your worker nodes can be only up to two versions behind the master version (n-2). The worker node IP address remains the same after the update operation.

input.speaker: user
input.text: Major. menor update.

input.speaker: agent
input.text: To determine the type of update that is available, compare your current worker node versions to the latest worker node fix pack version in the Red Hat OpenShift version change log. Major updates are indicated by the first digit in the version label (4.x.x), minor updates are indicated by the second digit (x.7.x).  A major.minor update moves up the Kubernetes version of the worker node to the same version as the master. This type of update often includes changes to the Kubernetes API or other behaviors that you must prepare your cluster for. Remember that your worker nodes can only be one version behind the master version (n-1). You can update the classic worker node to the same patch by using the ibmcloud oc worker update command.

input.text: A tag consists of multiple parts as X.Y.Z-.[hash] where X represents the major version of an image; Y represents the minor version of an image and Z represents an incremental ID that determines the latest patched minor version.Depending on the tag that you use, you must consider upgrading the logging agent image in your DevOps maintenance plan, to resolve vulnerabilities and apply agent enhancements and agent bug fixes. Moreover  Tags are case-sensitive, and the maximum length of a tag is 128 characters.  The characters that are permitted to name tags are A-Z, 0-9, spaces, underscore, hyphen, period, and colon. Colons turn the tag into a string where you can isolate two logical parts, like a key:value pair. You can't use a colon in a tag without creating this pairing. A comma separate tags and can't be used within the tag name itself. If you add PII information in the name, you might be disclosing sensitive data to others in the same account.When you define your tags, do not add sensitive information in the tag name.Tags are visible to all members of an account.To control tag visibility, circulate tagging guidelines, and let users know that tags are visible account-wide. input.text: There are general steps to gather the relevant worker node data: First,  Check the conditions of your worker nodes and cluster before you gather data. input.speaker: user
input.text: Worker node

input.speaker: agent
input.text: If you are asking about worker nodes With IBM Cloud Kubernetes Service, they are instances managed by your cluster through virtual machines. Those worker nodes virtual machines and all the worker node components are dedicated to you only and are not shared with other IBM customers .You are responsible to request timely updates to the worker nodes to ensure that the worker node OS and Red Hat OpenShift on IBM Cloud components apply the latest security updates and patches. Nevertheless the underlying hardware is shared with other IBM customers. Worker nodes carry the deployments and services that make up your app. When you host workloads in the public cloud, you want to ensure that your app is protected from being accessed, changed, or monitored by an unauthorized user or software Additionally worker nodes in standard clusters are provisioned in to your IBM Cloud infrastructure account.

input.speaker: agent
input.text: Red Hat OpenShift is a  Kubernetes container platform that provides a trusted environment to run enterprise workloads. It extends the Kubernetes platform with built-in software to enhance app lifecycle development, operations, and security. There is the Red Hat OpeShfit on IBM Cloud service, where  IBM sets up and helps you manage a cluster of worker nodes that come installed with the OpenShift Container Platform container orchestration software. Your Red Hat OpenShift workloads can scale across IBM’s global footprint of data centers and multizone regions. At the same time, you’re able to uniformly monitor, log, and secure apps. Additionally  is designed especially to accelerate your containerized app delivery processes that run on a Red Hat Enterprise Linux 7 operating system. You can orchestrate and scale your existing Red Hat OpenShift workloads across on-prem and off-prem clouds for a portable, hybrid solution that works the same in multicloud scenarios.

input.text: What are the MOXIE instrument's primary attributes and functionalities?

input.speaker: user
input.text: What are the MOXIE instrument's primary attributes and functionalities?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: The Mars Oxygen In-Situ Resource Utilization Experiment (MOXIE) is a Scale Model for a Future Big MOXIE, an instrument designed to help NASA prepare for human exploration of Mars. Its main job is to produce oxygen from the Martian carbon-dioxide atmosphere where less than 1% as much air on the surface as on Earth is present.  It will test a way for future explorers to produce oxygen for burning fuel and breathing. MOXIE is currently making 6-10 grams of oxygen per hour, but the Big MOXIE, would need to make 2,000 to 3,000 grams of oxygen per hour to support a crewed mission to Mars.

input.text: No, MOXIE does not work like a tree. It produces oxygen from the Martian carbon-dioxide atmosphere using a device called an "electrolyzer," a close cousin to a fuel cell that is running in reverse. On Earth, trees and plants absorb carbon dioxide and release oxygen through photosynthesis, but MOXIE uses a different process to achieve the same result.

The soft drink, Moxie, was invented in the late 1800s in Lowell, Massachusetts, not far from the M.I.T. Haystack Observatory where the MOXIE principal investigator works.  You can still buy Moxie in New England, and it's considered the official drink of the state of Maine. The connection with soft drinks doesn't end there. According to some accounts, the Moxie company was the first in the U.S. to use carbon dioxide (CO2) to make a beverage fizzy.  MOXIE is the first to use carbon dioxide on Mars.

The soft drink, Moxie, was invented in the late 1800s in Lowell, Massachusetts, not far from the M.I.T. Haystack Observatory where the MOXIE principal investigator works.  You can still buy Moxie in New England, and it's considered the official drink of the state of Maine. The connection with soft drinks doesn't end there. According to some accounts, the Moxie company was the first in the U.S. to use carbon dioxide (CO2) to make a beverage fizzy.  MOXIE is the first to use carbon dioxide on Mars.

input.speaker: user
input.text: Others in the past?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: There have been previous experiments and studies related to Mars exploration and resource utilization.  Some notable ones include the Mars Exploration Rovers, Sprit and Opportunity, which landed on Mars in 2004; the Mars Reconnaissance Orbiter, which has been studying Mars from orbit since 2006; the Mars Science Laboratory mission, which include the Curiosity rover that has been exploring Mars since 2012; and the InSight lander, which touched down on Mars in 2018 to study the planet's deep interior.  Additionally, there was the Mars Global Surveyor, which orbited Mars from 1997 to 2006, and Mars Pathfinder mission, which included the Sojourner rover and landed on Mars in 1997.  These are just few examples of the many missions and programs that have contributed to our understanding of Mars.

input.speaker: user
input.text: Can capital expenses for volunteer purposes be deducted from income?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: Indeed you can't deduct the value of your time or services, including the  value of income lost while you work as an unpaid volunteer for a qualified organization. You can only deduct  what something cost  even if you had done volunteer work for a charity as a sole prop you could only deduct your actual costs. If you paid an employee to do charity work or to learn something related to the business that would be deductible as a normal business expense. It would be impossible for the IRS to determine the value of your time and it would open the tax system to an enormous amount of fraud (think of being able to make up time spent or writing off time spent volunteering at a soup kitchen or any other charity). Nevertheless, you can write off expenses you have involved in doing the work, equipment and supplies used to do the work along with any wages you paid an employee or contractor to do said work.

input.text: Indeed you can't deduct the value of your time or services, including the  value of income lost while you work as an unpaid volunteer for a qualified organization. You can only deduct  what something cost  even if you had done volunteer work for a charity as a sole prop you could only deduct your actual costs. If you paid an employee to do charity work or to learn something related to the business that would be deductible as a normal business expense. It would be impossible for the IRS to determine the value of your time and it would open the tax system to an enormous amount of fraud (think of being able to make up time spent or writing off time spent volunteering at a soup kitchen or any other charity). input.speaker: user
input.text: In other words, charitable donations, capital expenses or my time could be make my taxesa lower, right?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: There are some tax-deductible options related to charitable activities. However, it's important to clarify that you cannot deduct the value of your time or services, including the income lost while working as an unpaid volunteer for a qualified organization. This includes charitable donations of time or services. However, you can deduct actual costs associated with volunteering, such as expenses for equipment, supplies, or wages paid to employees or contractors for work related to the charity. If you have donated items like a published book that you authored (not specifically written for the organization), you might be able to deduct its fair market value. In summary, while you cannot directly deduct the value of your time or services, you can deduct certain expenses related to charitable activities, which may help lower your tax burden.  In fact, tax deductions allow you to give to charities from your gross salary, not your net salary. The Charitable donations can be deducted from your income, and in that way make your taxable income lower, hence lower taxes.

No matter your net worth, it's important to have a basic estate plan in place. Such a plan ensures that your family and financial goals are met after you die. Beside having a good attorney , the will is included in your estate plan and you also should include  a living will or health-care proxy (medical power of attorney). Keep in mind when you do your estate plan and will  that there are different federal and state laws governing states. Take a  inventory of your assets (investments, retirement savings, insurance policies, and real estate or business interests).It is very important you have a trust, that are legal mechanisms that let you put conditions on how and when your assets will be distributed upon your death. They also allow you to reduce your estate and gift taxes and to distribute assets to your heirs without the cost, delay and publicity of probate court, which administers wills. Some also offer greater protection of your assets from creditors and lawsuits.

input.text: how does pre-market trading work?

input.speaker: user
input.text: how does pre-market trading work?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: Pre-market trading takes place before the regular trading hours (RTH) of the stock market. For example, pre-market hours are from 4:00 a.m. to 9:30 a.m. Eastern Time (ET) on the NASDAQ stock market. During pre-market hours, you can place limit orders, but you cannot place market orders due to lower liquidity. This is because the volume and liquidity are generally much lower during pre-market hours compared to RTH. If an unexpected world event occurs, the volume and liquidity might increase. It is important to note that not all brokers allow pre-market trading, and if they do, you might need to specify that you want your order to be executed during pre-market hours.

input.speaker: user
input.text: what are limit orders vs market orders?

input.speaker: agent
input.text:
A limit order is a type of order where you set a specific price at which you want to buy or sell a security. The order will only be executed if the market price reaches the price you set. On the other hand, a market order is an order to buy or sell a security immediately at the best available current market price. Market orders do not guarantee a specific price, and the final execution price may be different from the price quoted at the time of order entry.
The choice between limit orders and market orders depends on your trading goals and risk tolerance.

input.text: if I'm in the US how can I buy / sell on other countries markets?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: If you're in the US and want to buy or sell securities on other countries' markets, you generally have two options. Some large brokers offer trading access to popular equity markets in other countries, such as the Toronto Stock Exchange, Mexico Stock Exchange, or London Stock Exchange. One such broker is Interactive Brokers, offering many foreign markets (19 countries) for US-based investors, allowing you to trade all these local markets within one universal account, which can be very convenient. However, at many brokers or in less popular foreign markets, you typically must open an account with a broker in that country.

input.speaker: user
input.text: why isn't there just one global market? wouldn't that be easier?

input.speaker: agent
input.text:
Creating a single global market would be quite complex and challenging for several reasons. First, each country has its own exchange, which is closely tied to its local currency. Exchanges primarily support transactions and settlements in their local currency, and securities are denominated in a single currency. Cross-currency translation would complicate the trading process, as it would require additional steps and considerations.
Furthermore, citizens and businesses in most countries are restricted to having bank accounts in local currency, making it difficult or impossible to open a domestic bank account in a foreign-denominated currency. A public company headquartered in a given country is more likely to do business with local citizenry and businesses in that currency, and thus look for investors from the same pool, which generally means listing in local currency on an exchange in that country.

input.text:
Day trading is risky, according to several opinions. It is probably the most often tried and failed activity in the financial world. People think they can parlay $1,000 investment into $1,000,000 in a week with little or no knowledge on how to evaluate stocks and or companies. If it were that simple everyone would be making money hand over fist in the market.

Other things to consider are: 1) the need for systems that can access very fast data streams so that you can make moves quickly as trends start to develop in the markets, 2) you may be paying commissions that may greatly reduce or even eliminate gains, 3) you need to spend all day monitoring investments, and 4) tax implications, since all day-trading gains are taxed at ordinary income tax rates (unless you are using a tax-sheltered account like an IRA).

input.speaker: agent
input.text: No, you cannot do day trading with just $5,500. Based on the available information, if you're in the US, you're required to follow the SEC rules on pattern day trading. FINRA rules define a “pattern day trader” as any customer who executes four or more “day trades” within five business days, provided that the number of day trades represents more than six percent of the customer’s total trades in the margin account for that same five business day period. If you trip this rule, you'll be required to maintain $25,000 in a margin brokerage account. If you can't maintain the balance, your account will be locked.

input.text: Working at a startup can involve several risks. One of the primary risks is the possibility of the business failing, which could result in your shares becoming worthless. There is also a risk that if the startup succeeds, the financial rewards may not be evenly distributed among employees. Additionally, startups often require long working hours and high levels of responsibility, and there may be a risk of job security as the company grows and hires new employees.

2. Get your family involved

4. Consider crowdfunding as an alternative financing method.

input.speaker: user
input.text: what factors pay major impact on the credit score?

input.speaker: agent
input.text:
The five factors that have a major impact on your FICO credit score are:
1. Payment history: Late payments can negatively affect your score, while on-time payments can improve it.
2. Credit utilization: This refers to how much of your available credit you are currently using. Lower is better, but having 0% usage is not ideal either. Aiming for 10% or less is a good goal.
3. Credit history: This is based on the average age of all your accounts, including credit cards and loans. Older accounts are generally better for your score.
4. Credit mix: Having a diverse mix of credit types (e.g., credit cards, mortgages, auto loans) can positively impact your score.
5. New credit: Applying for new credit can result in a temporary decrease in your score due to the credit inquiries made during the application process. These inquiries remain on your report for two years.
It is important to note that errors and fraud can also impact your credit score, so regularly reviewing your credit reports from the three major credit bureaus (Experian, Equifax, and TransUnion) is recommended.

input.speaker: user
input.text: What do you mean by FICO score? I just check my credit score via credit karma. Is that the same?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: The FICO score is a type of credit score developed by the Fair Isaac Corporation. It is widely used by lenders to determine the creditworthiness of potential borrowers. Credit Karma, on the other hand, provides users with free access to their credit scores, which are typically based on the VantageScore model, developed by the three major credit bureaus (Experian, Equifax, and TransUnion). While FICO and VantageScore models have some similarities, they are not the same and may produce slightly different credit scores. Therefore, the credit score you see on Credit Karma might not be exactly the same as your FICO score. However, it can still provide you with a general idea of your credit standing.

input.speaker: user
input.text: What's the difference between Market Cap and NAV?

input.speaker: user
input.text: What's the difference between Market Cap and NAV?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: Market capitalization, or Market Cap, is calculated by multiplying the share price by the number of shares. It represents the current market perception of the company's worth. On the other hand, NAV, or net asset value, is the value of the company's stock divided by the number of shares, also known as the book value. NAV reflects the value of the company's assets if it were to sell everything, pay off its debts, and shut down. While Market Cap represents current market sentiment and future expectations, NAV provides a measure of the company's tangible asset value per share. These two values often differ significantly, especially for companies expected to grow or those with substantial intangible assets. NAV is a measurable value, derived from known factors, while Market Cap is determined by the stock price, which is influenced not only by NAV but also by future expectations.

Relying on luck in such transactions can be risky due to the high dollar amounts and liabilities involved. Here are some steps to consider before making a purchase:
1. Clarify your goals.
2. Work with trustworthy professionals.
3. Establish a budget.
4. Be prepared to handle unexpected surprises in the process.
The key to successful investing is understanding what you are investing in. For instance, if you are buying stock in a company that produces product X, it's important to comprehend the business. It's advisable to invest in good companies, but it's also crucial to do so at good prices. This requires understanding metrics such as price-to-earnings, price-to-revenue, and price-to-book ratios.

Relying on luck in such transactions can be risky due to the high dollar amounts and liabilities involved. Here are some steps to consider before making a purchase:
1. Clarify your goals.
2. Work with trustworthy professionals.
3. Establish a budget.
4. Be prepared to handle unexpected surprises in the process.
The key to successful investing is understanding what you are investing in. For instance, if you are buying stock in a company that produces product X, it's important to comprehend the business. It's advisable to invest in good companies, but it's also crucial to do so at good prices. This requires understanding metrics such as price-to-earnings, price-to-revenue, and price-to-book ratios.

input.speaker: user
input.text: Will purchasing stocks give me control over it?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: You will own a fraction of the company that you buy. You must buy 100% of the outstanding stock to own the company entirely.  Purchasing most of the shares allows you to control the company. The process may be complex, and you may need to appoint board members at preset intervals. The company's charter may include conditions to prevent a change in control. Depending on your jurisdiction, owning a certain percentage of shares may come with specific requirements.

input.speaker: user
input.text: Value?

input.speaker: user
input.text: What's the difference between Market Cap and NAV?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: Market capitalization, or Market Cap, is calculated by multiplying the share price by the number of shares. It represents the current market perception of the company's worth. On the other hand, NAV, or net asset value, is the value of the company's stock divided by the number of shares, also known as the book value. NAV reflects the value of the company's assets if it were to sell everything, pay off its debts, and shut down. While Market Cap represents current market sentiment and future expectations, NAV provides a measure of the company's tangible asset value per share. These two values often differ significantly, especially for companies expected to grow or those with substantial intangible assets. NAV is a measurable value, derived from known factors, while Market Cap is determined by the stock price, which is influenced not only by NAV but also by future expectations.

input.speaker: agent
input.text: The concept of value is a subject where economic theories often differ, and each school of thought has valid points. One school of thought argues that monetary value can always be determined by the net labor involved or the cost of inputs. The other school of thought suggests that economic value does not exist inherently and that only the market can determine value. For instance, the value of a stock is solely determined by the price people are willing to pay for it and the price at which those who have it are willing to sell. Even if something drastic happens to a company, such as their only factory burning down and traders remaining indifferent, the value of the stock would not change in that scenario.

input.text: Good decisions

input.speaker: agent
input.text: Any decisions a business makes are for one reason: $$$. Decision-makers must CONSTANTLY re-evaluate not only the current climate but the future.  If business owners aren't careful, it very well could collapse, and in the end, if it fails, nobody else is responsible except themselves. So, in times of success, yes, the business owners reap profit, but in tough times, they have to take full responsibility and the consequences of reality. You can't run a business without making " $$$." Good will and good intentions only go so far, and they do not always equate to positive results. Capitalism rewards the positive results of proper decision-making in the marketplace as businesses generate enough profit to allow them to stay afloat and expand, while those businesses that do not follow through with appropriate decision-making reap negative results, fail, and eventually go out of business.

input.speaker: user
input.text: What about employees?

input.speaker: agent
input.text:
As your company grows, you tend to add people in "layers." The top layer is the first founder or founder. There may be one, two, three, or more of you, but you all start working at about the same time, and you all take the same risk: quitting your jobs to work for a new and unproven company. 
The second layer is the first actual employees. By the time you hire this layer, you've got cash coming in from somewhere (investors or customers—doesn't matter). These people didn't take as much risk because they got a salary from day one, and honestly, they didn't start the company, they joined it as a job. The third layer consists of later employees. By the time they joined the company, it was going pretty well. Each "layer" will be approximately one year for many companies. When your company is big enough to sell to Google or go public, you probably have about six layers: the founders and roughly five layers of employees. Each successive layer is more significant. It's very important to keep your employees happy and ensure they feel part of something other than a job. Make them feel like they're part of a family and support system.

input.text: Why sell?

input.speaker: user
input.text: I cannot get select query to work, what steps should I take?

input.speaker: user
input.text: I cannot get select query to work, what steps should I take?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: 1. Check if you have the correct access rights: You need at least 'Reader' access to the buckets that contain the input objects. 2. After the FROM keyword, specify one or more unique resource identifiers (URIs) which can be thought of as tables. Each URI can specify one or more input objects, which can also be thought of as table partitions. 3. If the format of the input objects is CSV, and no special options are required, it is not necessary to specify a STORED AS clause. However, if the format is JSON, ORC, Parquet, or AVRO, after the FROM clause, specify STORED AS JSON, STORED AS ORC, STORED AS PARQUET, or STORED AS AVRO. 4. If text formats, such as JSON and CSV, are compressed with either gzip or bzip2 and have the extensions .gz and .bz, they automatically get recognized as compressed files. However, do not use these kinds of compressed files due to performance reasons. 5. If the format of the input objects is CSV and a delimiter other than the default , (comma) is used, you must specify the delimiter by using the FIELDS TERMINATED BY option of the STORED AS clause. All single Unicode characters are allowed as delimiters. 6. Check for a header line: By default, it is assumed that CSV input objects have a header line that specifies the names of the input columns.

input.speaker: user
input.text: how can I improve query

input.speaker: agent
input.text: You can use the tools that are built into Discovery to make improvements. If you want more control over how queries are handled, you must use the query API. Cataloging improves your query performance, especially for text-based data formats where the schema inference requires a full scan of the data before the actual query execution. Adding a custom stop words list can improve the relevance of results for natural language queries. You can also improve the quality of search results by expanding the meaning of the queries that are submitted by customers.

input.speaker: user
input.text: can you give more details about the query API?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: Watson Query provides REST APIs that you can use to interact with your instance. When you submit a search from the product user interface, your text is passed as a natural language query value to the Query API. You can use operators when you write queries to submit to Discovery by using the Query API. The API coordination node can make a request directly to servers that host that data rather than needing to combine responses from many servers. The API coordination node is also free from buffering the response since it has no combination step to carry out. As a result, the data arrives at the client more quickly.

input.speaker: user
input.text: Can I address my storage space limits? how can I add or delete storage?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: Yes, you can take the following actions to free up space: 1. Delete snapshot versions of any models that you do not expect to need to revert to. 2. Delete any models that you do not need. 3. If your models are too important to delete, consider upgrading your plan to one that provides a larger allotment of storage space. 4. After you remove models or model versions, wait an hour before you retry the action that resulted in the error message. Regarding add or delete storage question: In the VMware Solutions console, click Resources > VMware as a Service from the left navigation pane. In the VMware as a Service table, click the Cloud director sites tab, then click an instance name.. Click the Infrastructure tab. On the Clusters tab, click a cluster name to expand its details. Click Edit NFS storage. In the Edit shared storage window, increase or decrease the current sizings for the performance tiers available. input.text: I cannot get select query to work, what steps should I take?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: 1. Check if you have the correct access rights: You need at least 'Reader' access to the buckets that contain the input objects. 2. After the FROM keyword, specify one or more unique resource identifiers (URIs) which can be thought of as tables. Each URI can specify one or more input objects, which can also be thought of as table partitions. 3. If the format of the input objects is CSV, and no special options are required, it is not necessary to specify a STORED AS clause. However, if the format is JSON, ORC, Parquet, or AVRO, after the FROM clause, specify STORED AS JSON, STORED AS ORC, STORED AS PARQUET, or STORED AS AVRO. 4. If text formats, such as JSON and CSV, are compressed with either gzip or bzip2 and have the extensions .gz and .bz, they automatically get recognized as compressed files. However, do not use these kinds of compressed files due to performance reasons. 5. If the format of the input objects is CSV and a delimiter other than the default , (comma) is used, you must specify the delimiter by using the FIELDS TERMINATED BY option of the STORED AS clause. All single Unicode characters are allowed as delimiters. 6. Check for a header line: By default, it is assumed that CSV input objects have a header line that specifies the names of the input columns.

input.text: how can I improve query

input.speaker: agent
input.text: You can use the tools that are built into Discovery to make improvements. If you want more control over how queries are handled, you must use the query API. Cataloging improves your query performance, especially for text-based data formats where the schema inference requires a full scan of the data before the actual query execution. Adding a custom stop words list can improve the relevance of results for natural language queries. You can also improve the quality of search results by expanding the meaning of the queries that are submitted by customers.

input.text: can you give more details about the query API?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: Watson Query provides REST APIs that you can use to interact with your instance. When you submit a search from the product user interface, your text is passed as a natural language query value to the Query API. You can use operators when you write queries to submit to Discovery by using the Query API. The API coordination node can make a request directly to servers that host that data rather than needing to combine responses from many servers. The API coordination node is also free from buffering the response since it has no combination step to carry out. As a result, the data arrives at the client more quickly.

input.text: how to create schema or tables?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: Here are the steps to create a schema and tables: To create a schema: 1. Go to Databases. 2. Select the database in which you want to create a schema. 3. Click Create schema. 4. Type a name for the schema. 5. Specify the retention time interval (in days) for the schema. 6. Click Create. To create a table: 1. Log in to IBM® watsonx.data console. 2. From the navigation menu, select Data manager. 3. Select the engine from the Engine menu. Catalogs that are associated with the selected engine are listed. 4. Click Create and select Create table. 5. In the Create table form, drag a file to the box or click to upload. 6. Click the data type and choose the required data types for each column. Click Next. 7. In the Target form, select the Engine, Catalog, and Schema in which the table is created. 8. Enter a name for the table in the Table name field and click Next. 9. Verify the details in the Summary page and scroll down to view the DDL preview. 10. Click Create. 11. Verify that the table creation status in the Result set is successful, indicated as true. 12. Go to the Data manager page and select the schema under which you created the table and click the refresh icon. The newly created table is listed.

input.text: How do I find specific conversations?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: To find specific conversations, you can use the time period control to choose the period for which data is displayed. This control affects all data shown on the page, including the list of conversations. You can choose to view data for a single day, a week, a month, or a quarter. However, please note that the statistics can cover a longer time period than the period for which logs of conversations are retained. For each entity you can select from the Values column to see a list of the most common values that were identified for this entity during the time period.

input.text: What is a strong password?

input.speaker: agent
input.text:
A strong password typically includes a combination of uppercase and lowercase letters, numbers, and special characters. It should be at least 12 characters long and not contain common phrases or easily guessable information like your name or birthdate. It's also recommended to avoid using the same password for multiple accounts. Must be at least 8 characters. (^.{8,}$)
Must have one number, one lowercase letter, and one capital letter. (^(?:(?=.d)(?=.[a-z])(?=.[A-Z]).)$)
Must have only English letters and numbers. (^[A-Za-z0-9]$)
Must have at least one unique character. (^(w)w?(?!1)w+$)

input.text: Celts

input.speaker: agent
input.text: Archaeological discoveries suggests that the pre-Roman Celtic societies were linked to the network of overland trade routes that spanned Eurasia but scholars assumed that the original land of the Celts was west of the Rhine , more precisely in Gaul , because it was where Greek and Roman ancient sources , namely Caesar , located the Celts . This view was challenged by the 19th - century historian Marie Henri d'Arbois de Jubainville who placed the land of origin of the Celts east of the Rhine.  Jubainville based his arguments on a phrase of Herodotus ' that placed the Celts at the source of the Danube , and argued that Herodotus had meant to place the Celtic homeland in southern Germany . The finding of the prehistoric cemetery of Hallstat in 1846 by Johan Ramsauer and the finding of the archaeological site of La Tène by Hansli Kopp in 1857 drew attention to this area. Archaeologists have also discovered large prehistoric trackways crossing bogs in Ireland and Germany . As the territory held by the Celts contained tin , lead , iron , silver and gold , they were metalworkers that created weapons and jewellery for international trade , particularly with the Romans .

This view was challenged by the 19th - century historian Marie Henri d'Arbois de Jubainville who placed the land of origin of the Celts east of the Rhine.  Jubainville based his arguments on a phrase of Herodotus ' that placed the Celts at the source of the Danube , and argued that Herodotus had meant to place the Celtic homeland in southern Germany . The finding of the prehistoric cemetery of Hallstat in 1846 by Johan Ramsauer and the finding of the archaeological site of La Tène by Hansli Kopp in 1857 drew attention to this area. As the territory held by the Celts contained tin , lead , iron , silver and gold , they were metalworkers that created weapons and jewellery for international trade , particularly with the Romans .

input.text: where is bone marrow found what does it do for the body

input.speaker: agent
input.text: Bone marrow is found within the spongy or cancellous portions of bones. Human marrow produces approximately 500 billion blood cells per day. The main functional component of bone marrow includes the progenitor cells which are destined to mature into blood and lymphoid cells. These include white blood cells, red blood cells, and platelets. In humans, the red marrow of some bones is an important site for blood cell production, in order to replace existing cells that have been destroyed by the liver.
