input.speaker: user
input.text: What happens if it does not work well?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: If bone marrow does not function properly, it can lead to a decrease in the production of blood cells and blood platelets. This can be caused by various factors such as aplastic anemia, malignancies like multiple myeloma, congenital defects , autoimmune diseases, infections like tuberculosis, leukemia, or exposure to radiation or chemotherapy. Symptoms of a malfunctioning bone marrow can include fatigue, increased risk of infection, and easy bruising or bleeding. In the case of sickle cell disease, the bone marrow's attempt to compensate for the destruction of red cells is not sufficient, leading to anemia.

input.speaker: user
input.text: How is Sickle cell treated?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: The treatment for sickle cell disease may include infection prevention with vaccination and antibiotics, high fluid intake, folic acid supplementation, pain medication, blood transfusion, and the medication hydroxycarbamide (hydroxyurea). A small percentage of people can be cured by a transplant of bone marrow cells. When treating avascular necrosis of the bone in people with sickle cell disease, the aim of treatment is to reduce or stop the pain and maintain joint mobility. Current treatment options are to rest the joint, physical therapy, pain relief medicine, joint replacement surgery, or bone grafting. High quality randomized controlled trials are needed to assess the most effective treatment option and determine if a combination of physical therapy and surgery are more effective than physical therapy alone.

input.speaker: user
input.text: How about transplant?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: A bone marrow transplant is a potential cure for sickle cell disease, although it is difficult to obtain a suitable donor due to the specific HLA typing necessary. Ideally, a close relative would donate the bone marrow. Risks associated with the procedure are a 7% mortality rate and the possibility of graft versus host disease. In phase 1 clinical trials for adults with severe sickle cell disease, the safety and initial evidence for efficacy have been assessed, no randomized controlled trials have been reported as of 2016. It is important to note that while a bone marrow transplant can potentially cure sickle cell disease, it is not a guaranteed solution and carries significant risks.

input.speaker: user
input.text: Is Huntington's disease also inherited?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: Yes, Huntington's disease is an inherited disorder from one's parents. It is caused by an autosomal dominant mutation in the Huntingtin gene, which results in an abnormal protein that gradually damages cells in the brain. If a person inherits the mutated gene from an affected parent, they will typically develop the disease. The probability of inheriting the mutant allele and being affected with the disorder is 50% for each offspring of an affected individual, regardless of sex.

input.speaker: user
input.text: Speed limit

input.speaker: agent
input.text: The law sets certain prima facie limits, including a speed limit of 25 MPH in business and residential districts, a speed limit of 15 MPH in alleys, at blind intersections, and blind railroad crossings, and a part-time speed limit of 25 MPH in school zones when children are going to and from school. Signs only sometimes indicate these speed limits, so all California motorists must be aware of these 10, 20, and 30-mile-per-hour speed laws. Local authorities can establish intermediate speed limits between 25 and 55 miles per hour based on traffic engineering surveys, which evaluate roadway conditions, accident records, and the prevailing speed of prudent drivers.

input.text: What about bicycles?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: In California, there are over 4,000 freeways, with approximately 1,000 miles open to bicyclists, mostly in rural areas with no alternate routes. According to California Vehicle Code Section 21960, Caltrans and local agencies have the authority to prohibit bicyclists from traveling on freeways under their jurisdiction, and they are required to post signs stating the prohibition. Currently, there are no signs permitting bicyclists on freeways. When bicyclists legally travel on a freeway, they may be directed off at the next off-ramp by a sign that says "Bicycles Must Exit."  The CHP has created a Bicycle Safety Course which holds valuable information related to bicycle safety and maintenance.

input.text: Are the laws the same for pedestrians?

input.speaker: agent
input.text:
No, they are not. The pedestrian signal, when activated, provides time for the pedestrian to enter the street on the steady "Walk" and to finish crossing the street on the flashing "Don't Walk" signal. The pedestrian signal is normally activated by a push-button that tells the signal controller that there is a pedestrian who wishes to cross the street.
Drivers and pedestrians must work together to demonstrate safe road behaviors, helping protect themselves and those around them. To ensure safety, pedestrians should make themselves visible by wearing bright-colored clothes and carrying a flashlight when walking at night. They should avoid dangerous behaviors, such as jaywalking, staying sober, and making eye contact with drivers. Pedestrians should also refrain from using their phones while walking, as talking and texting can distract them from paying attention to their surroundings.
Before crossing a street, pedestrians should look before stepping, cross streets at marked crosswalks or intersections, obey traffic signals, and watch for turning vehicles. It's important to look left-right-left before crossing a street.

input.text: Can you tell me the greatest pandemics worldwide before  COVID in 2020?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: Yes, a number of pandemics have affected the world before COVID. The 1918 flu pandemic , which lasted until 1919, was a category 5 influenza pandemic caused by an unusually severe and deadly influenza A virus . The victims were healthy young adults , in contrast to most influenza outbreaks , which predominantly affect juvenile , elderly , or otherwise - weakened patients . Older estimates say it killed 40 -- 50 million people , while more recent research suggests that it may have killed as many as 100 million people , or 5 % of the world 's population in 1918. The third bubonic plague pandemic, also known as the modern pandemic, spread the bubonic plague disease to port cities throughout the world in the second half of the 19th century and early 20th century via shipping routes . The bubonic plague affected people in Chinatown in San Francisco from 1900 - 1904, and the people of Oakland and east bay again from 1907 - 1909. In 1994, Zimbabwe suffered outbreaks of acute diseases like plague. In 2014, West African had the Ebola virus epidemic. In 2001 the Bubonic plague affected Zambia , India , Malawi , Algeria , China , Peru , and the Democratic Republic of the Congo .   2009 saw a pandemic involving the H1N1 influenza virus (swine flu). These are just some examples of other modern-day pandemics.

input.text: How did  HIV originated and where did start the first cases?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: The origin of HIV is still a matter of debate among scientists, but it is believed originated in sub-Saharan Africa (Southeast Cameroon) through exposure to infected blood and body fluids while butchering bushmeat during the 20th century , although  symptomatic infections were not reported until the 1980s. AIDS was at first considered a disease of gay men and drug addicts but then in Africa it took off among the general population . AIDS became a significant problem for the region , particularly in Côte d'Ivoire , Liberia , and Nigeria . The onset of the HIV epidemic in the region began in 1985 with reported cases in Benin and Nigeri , and in nearby countries, such as Côte d'Ivoire, in subsequent years .

input.text: Is there a cure for HIV?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: With proper medical care, HIV can be controlled. It was reported in  October 2011 that  the anti-HIV drug tenofovir , when used topically in a microbicidal vaginal gel  reduce herpes virus sexual transmission by 51 % . Also  a genetic approach involving intrabodies that block CCR5 expression has been proposed as a treatment for HIV - 1 infected individuals . Some good results were reported in 2014 when enrollment of HIV - positive patients in a clinical trial was started in 2009 in which the patients ' cells were genetically modified with a zinc finger nuclease to carry the CCR5 - Δ32 trait and then reintroduced into the body as a potential HIV treatment .

input.text: Is Herpes a lethal disease and how is it transmitted?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: Herpes is not generally considered a lethal disease .There are two types of herpes simplex virus , type 1 ( HSV - 1 ) and type 2 ( HSV - 2 ) . HSV - 1 more commonly causes infections around the mouth while HSV - 2 more commonly causes genital infections.Herpes is contracted through direct contact with an active lesion or body fluid of an infected person . Herpes transmission occurs between discordant partners; a person with a history of infection ( HSV seropositive ) can pass the virus to an HSV seronegative person . Herpes simplex virus type 2 is typically contracted through direct skin - to - skin contact with an infected individual , but can also be contracted by exposure to infected saliva , semen , vaginal fluid , or the fluid from herpetic blisters. However , asymptomatic carriers of the HSV - 2 virus are still contagious . In many infections , the first symptom people will have of their own infections is the horizontal transmission to a sexual partner or the vertical transmission of neonatal herpes to a newborn at term . Since most asymptomatic individuals are unaware of their infection , they are considered at high risk for spreading HSV .

input.text: how does the mls all star game work?

input.speaker: user
input.text: how does the mls all star game work?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: The MLS All-Star Game is an annual soccer game where selected players from Major League Soccer (MLS) compete against an international club. The format of the game has evolved over the years. Initially, it featured the Eastern Conference playing against the Western Conference. However, since 2005, MLS has been inviting a professional club from abroad to participate. The opponents are usually from Europe, specifically England's Premier League, due to the scheduling coincidence of the MLS midseason with the pre-seasons of most European leagues. Players earn roster spots through a combination of fan voting and selections by the appointed manager and league commissioner. The game is considered a pre-season friendly from a European perspective.

input.text: American Football is the most popular spectator sport in the United States by several measures. It has a large following and attracts significant television audiences. American Football is also played and viewed in other countries. For instance, American football has a long history in Mexico , which was introduced to the sport in 1896 . It  was the second-most popular sport in Mexico in the 1950s , with the game being particularly popular in colleges . The Los Angeles Times notes that the NFL claims over 16 million fans in Mexico , which places the country third behind the US and Canada. The game has a significant following in Canada according to a 2013 poll , 21 % of respondents said they followed the NFL very closely '' or fairly closely '' , making it the third-most followed league behind the National Hockey League ( NHL ) and Canadian Football League ( CFL ). The sport is played in European countries as well, such as Switzerland , which has American football clubs in every major city , and Germany . But in those countries American football has not yet reached the same level of popularity it has in North America.

input.text: Soccer, also known as football in many parts of the world, is a sport where two teams of 11 players each try to score goals by kicking a ball into the opposing team's net between two goalposts. Nevertheless, in the American football, also know gridiron , is a team sport played by two teams of eleven players on a rectangular field with goalposts at each end . The offense , which is the team controlling the oval - shaped football , attempts to advance down the field by running with or passing the ball , while the defense , which is the team without control of the ball , aims to stop the offense 's advance and aims to take control of the ball for themselves . The offense must advance at least ten yards in four downs , or plays , and otherwise they turn over the football to the defense ; if the offense succeeds in advancing ten yards or more , they are given a new set of four downs . Points are primarily scored by advancing the ball into the opposing team 's end zone for a touchdown or kicking the ball through the opponent 's goalposts for a field goal . The team with the most points at the end of a game wins .

The goal or line being defended by the opposing team and the players only can use their body to move the ball. Nevertheless, in the American football, also know gridiron , is a team sport played by two teams of eleven players on a rectangular field with goalposts at each end . The offense , which is the team controlling the oval - shaped football , attempts to advance down the field by running with or passing the ball , while the defense , which is the team without control of the ball , aims to stop the offense 's advance and aims to take control of the ball for themselves . The offense must advance at least ten yards in four downs , or plays , and otherwise they turn over the football to the defense ; if the offense succeeds in advancing ten yards or more , they are given a new set of four downs . Points are primarily scored by advancing the ball into the opposing team 's end zone for a touchdown or kicking the ball through the opponent 's goalposts for a field goal . The team with the most points at the end of a game wins .

input.text: To file a Civil Harassment Restraining Order in Sacramento Superior Court, you need to complete the Request for Civil Harassment Restraining Order (Step 1) Packet (CV\E-CH-01) and present the completed forms to the Civil Front Counter - Window 14 in Room 102 of the Gordon D. Schaber Sacramento County Courthouse. You will need to pay a filing fee, but if you cannot afford it, you can request a fee waiver. After filing, if your forms are completed and filed by 11:30 a.m., you will receive your Order at 4:00 p.m. the same day. If the forms are filed after 11:30 a.m., then the Order will be ready for pick up after 4:00 p.m. the next court business day. It is your responsibility to have the respondent personally served no later than five (5) days prior to the scheduled hearing date. Proof of service for the respondent must be filed with the court prior to or at the time of the Civil Harassment Restraining Order hearing.

input.speaker: user
input.text: In the US, does getting a loan with a cosigner, help your credit rating?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: In the US getting a loan with a cosigner, it can potentially help your credit rating, but it depends on how the loan is structured and reported. However a loan with a cosigner can bring a lot problems. In the first scenario, if the loan company reports the status to a credit reporting agency on behalf of both the initiating borrower and the cosigner, then both individuals get a new account on their credit report. Initially, this might decrease related credit scores slightly due to a hard pull, new account with zero history, and increased debt. However, over time, it can have a positive effect on both people's credit ratings if the loan is paid properly. Conversely, if the loan is not paid properly, it will negatively affect both people's credit. In the second scenario, if the loan company only reports to a credit agency on one of you (probably the cosigner), leaving the other as just a backup, it will have no effect on the borrower's credit. In the third scenario, if the loan company doesn't report the loan at all to a credit agency, there will be no direct impact on the borrower's credit. However, if the loan goes into collections and is reported as such, it would negatively affect the borrower's credit. Nevertheless a cosigner will make sure you are going to make every single payment on time because otherwise he will be responsible to pay it  .

input.speaker: user
input.text: FICO?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: FICO is financial services company, whose customers are financial services companies. The purpose of the credit score system is two-fold. First, the credit score is intended to make it easy for lending institutions (FICO's customers) to assess the risk of loans that they make.  The second purpose of the credit score is to incentivize consumers into borrowing money. FICO scoring prefers to see a large number of accounts, low utilization, high average account age, low number of inquiries, no late payment.A credit card can be a long running line of credit that will help to boost your FICO score. However if you have student loans, a mortgage, or car payments those will work just as well.  FICO that stands for Fair Isaac Corporation uses information from the 3 major credit bureaus   Equifax, Transunion and Experian. It is important to note that FICO score tracks credit not checking or savings.

input.text: What does a small claims court do

input.speaker: user
input.text: What does a small claims court do

input.speaker: agent
input.text: A small claims court is designed to provide a fair, quick, and inexpensive way for individuals to resolve disputes involving relatively small amounts of money. You must have asked the defendant to pay and the defendant has refused. You may not have a lawyer represent you. The judge will hear your case or the court will give parties an opportunity to meet with a mediator at the time of trial. The Small Claims Court has a monetary limit on the amount of money damages that can be claimed, usually up to $12,500 for "a natural person", with some exceptions.

input.text: Yes, if you are at least 18 years old, you can file a small claims court case for the additional $1000, as Small Claims cases are for damages of $10,000 or less.  You will need to bring proof to to your court date to show to the other side. Examples of proof (also called evidence) are: Repair receipts, Photos of damage, Emails.

input.speaker: user
input.text: What does a small claims court do

input.speaker: agent
input.text: A small claims court is designed to provide a fair, quick, and inexpensive way for individuals to resolve disputes involving relatively small amounts of money. You must have asked the defendant to pay and the defendant has refused. You may not have a lawyer represent you. The judge will hear your case or the court will give parties an opportunity to meet with a mediator at the time of trial. The Small Claims Court has a monetary limit on the amount of money damages that can be claimed, usually up to $12,500 for "a natural person", with some exceptions.

input.text: In Ventura County the filing fee for a small claims case depends on the amount of your claim: * If your claim is for $1,500.00 or less, the filing fee is $30.00. * If your claim is more than $1,500.00 but less than or equal to $5,000.00, the filing fee is $50.00. * If your claim is more than $5,000.00 but less than or equal to $10,000.00, the filing fee is $75.00. * If you have filed more than 12 small claims in California within the past 12 months, the filing fee is $100.00. input.speaker: user
input.text: How about mediation?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: You can mediate your case before or after a small claims case has been filed. If you mediate before filing a case and an agreement is reached at the mediation, there will be no need to file a small claims case. If a case has already been filed, the agreement reached at the mediation can be entered into the court record without having to appear in court. The Superior Court of California  provides free mediation services for litigants in small claims cases. The mediation is facilitated by trained volunteer mediators. The benefits of mediation include determining the outcome of the case by the parties themselves, saving important relationships without going to court, and having both views of the dispute heard. If the case is settled after the filing of the claim and before the hearing date, the plaintiff must file Judicial Council form “Request For Dismissal” (CIV-110) with the court.

input.text: The defendant and I live in different counties, where do I sue?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: If the defendant and you live in different counties, you can sue in the county where the defendant lives or where the business involved is located, where the damage or accident happened, where the contract was signed or carried out, or if the defendant is a corporation, where the contract was broken. For a retail installment account or sales contract or a motor vehicle finance sale, you can also sue in the county where the property was bought, even if the defendant does not live in that county. If you file your claim in the wrong county, the court will dismiss the claim.

input.text: Using credit card points to pay for tax deductible business expenses

input.speaker: user
input.text: Using credit card points to pay for tax deductible business expenses

input.speaker: agent
input.text: When using credit card points to pay for tax-deductible business expenses, the expenses are not actually deductible because the points used effectively cancel out the cost. However, if the points were earned on a personal credit card and used for business-related expenses, the employer can reimburse the employee for those expenses, and it seems the IRS follows an unofficial "don't ask, don't tell" rule regarding any rewards or cashback earned on the employee's personal card. It's important to note that credit card fees for personal expenses are not tax-deductible, while fees for business credit cards are deductible on Schedule C or the appropriate form for reporting business income and expenses. If you have a card used for both business and personal expenses, it's advisable to get separate cards for clarity and ease of accounting. In summary, while the expenses themselves may not be deductible when using credit card points, the employer can reimburse the employee for the expenses, and having separate cards for business and personal use is recommended, i.e., getting a personal card that you only use for business expenses,  if you don't opt for a business card. It makes it very clear that expenses on that card are business expenses, and is just more professional.

input.text: open a small business

input.speaker: agent
input.text: Opening a small business is the biggest dream of most entrepreneurs nowadays. The path can be of different forms and include starting one from scratch, buying an existing one or opting for a franchise. There are rules, regulations, requirements and laws for legally  operating  a small business, some of these may be federal but for the most part, it will be the locality (state, county, city) of the business that determines what needs to be done, including with regard to income taxes. It is best to start out creating a small business where you can control the content and grow it so that it ends up being a fairly decent-paying job for yourself and maybe a few others. Jobs for average people are disappearing, but those with the required dedication and discipline can scratch out an alright living running their own show.

input.text: If you are asking about register a small business name, it is important to note that their names are notoriously hard to protect when they are active. Your business will be treated as a sole proprietorship. If your revenue exceeds 30,000 (or wish to collect GST for the government) then you will have to register with the CRA for a GST account, but that is free.

input.text: How can I delete a key?

input.speaker: user
input.text: How can I delete a key?

input.speaker: agent
input.text:
To delete a key using IBM Key Protect, you can follow these steps:
1. Log in to the IBM Cloud console.
2. Go to Menu > Resource List to view a list of your resources.
3. From your IBM Cloud resource list, select your provisioned instance of Key Protect.
4. On the application details page, use the Keys table to browse the keys in your service.
5. Click the ⋯ icon to open a list of options for the key that you want to delete.
Please note that when you delete a key, it is "soft deleted", meaning that the key and its associated data will be restorable up to 30 days after deletion. After 90 days, the key becomes eligible to be automatically purged, or hard deleted, and its associated data will be permanently removed from the Key Protect service.
Also, keep in mind that deleting a key will make any data encrypted by the key inaccessible. If the key is restored within the 30-day time frame, this can be reversed. However, after 30 days, key metadata, registrations, and policies are available for up to 90 days, at which point the key becomes eligible to be purged. Once a key is no longer restorable and has been purged, its associated data can no longer be accessed.

input.text:
To delete a key using IBM Key Protect, you can follow these steps:
1. Log in to the IBM Cloud console.
2. Go to Menu > Resource List to view a list of your resources.
3. From your IBM Cloud resource list, select your provisioned instance of Key Protect.
4. On the application details page, use the Keys table to browse the keys in your service.
5. Click the ⋯ icon to open a list of options for the key that you want to delete.
Please note that when you delete a key, it is "soft deleted", meaning that the key and its associated data will be restorable up to 30 days after deletion. After 90 days, the key becomes eligible to be automatically purged, or hard deleted, and its associated data will be permanently removed from the Key Protect service.
Also, keep in mind that deleting a key will make any data encrypted by the key inaccessible. If the key is restored within the 30-day time frame, this can be reversed. Once a key is no longer restorable and has been purged, its associated data can no longer be accessed.

input.speaker: user
input.text: what happen when the first user authorizes a key for deletion?

input.speaker: agent
input.text:
When the first user authorizes a key for deletion, it remains in the Active state for seven days. During this time, all key operations are allowed on the key. To complete the deletion, another user with a Manager role can use the Key Protect GUI or API to delete the key at any point during those seven days. Once the key is deleted, it will be moved to the Destroyed state. However, because it is impossible to purge an active key, another user must delete the key before it can be purged.
The key and its associated data will be inaccessible 90 days after being deleted. When you delete a key, it is "soft deleted", meaning that the key and its associated data will be restorable up to 30 days after deletion. You are able to still retrieve associated data such as key metadata, registrations, and policies for up to 90 days. After 90 days, the key becomes eligible to be automatically purged, or hard deleted, and its associated data will be permanently removed from the Key Protect service.

input.text: What could happen if no action is taken by the second user and the 7-day period experies?

input.speaker: user
input.text: What could happen if no action is taken by the second user and the 7-day period experies?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: If no action is taken by the second user and the 7-day period expires, the key will remain in the Active state and will not be deleted and you must restart the dual authorization process to delete the key . All key operations will still be allowed on the key. The key and its associated data will continue to be accessible and will not be moved to the Destroyed state. No further action can be taken to delete the key until the first user authorizes deletion again.

input.speaker: user
input.text: How can I delete a key?

input.speaker: agent
input.text:
To delete a key using IBM Key Protect, you can follow these steps:
1. Log in to the IBM Cloud console.
2. Go to Menu > Resource List to view a list of your resources.
3. From your IBM Cloud resource list, select your provisioned instance of Key Protect.
4. On the application details page, use the Keys table to browse the keys in your service.
5. Click the ⋯ icon to open a list of options for the key that you want to delete.
Please note that when you delete a key, it is "soft deleted", meaning that the key and its associated data will be restorable up to 30 days after deletion. After 90 days, the key becomes eligible to be automatically purged, or hard deleted, and its associated data will be permanently removed from the Key Protect service.
Also, keep in mind that deleting a key will make any data encrypted by the key inaccessible. If the key is restored within the 30-day time frame, this can be reversed. However, after 30 days, key metadata, registrations, and policies are available for up to 90 days, at which point the key becomes eligible to be purged. Once a key is no longer restorable and has been purged, its associated data can no longer be accessed.

input.speaker: user
input.text: what happen when the first user authorizes a key for deletion?

input.speaker: agent
input.text:
When the first user authorizes a key for deletion, it remains in the Active state for seven days. During this time, all key operations are allowed on the key. To complete the deletion, another user with a Manager role can use the Key Protect GUI or API to delete the key at any point during those seven days. Once the key is deleted, it will be moved to the Destroyed state. However, because it is impossible to purge an active key, another user must delete the key before it can be purged.
The key and its associated data will be inaccessible 90 days after being deleted. When you delete a key, it is "soft deleted", meaning that the key and its associated data will be restorable up to 30 days after deletion. You are able to still retrieve associated data such as key metadata, registrations, and policies for up to 90 days. After 90 days, the key becomes eligible to be automatically purged, or hard deleted, and its associated data will be permanently removed from the Key Protect service.

input.speaker: user
input.text: What could happen if no action is taken by the second user and the 7-day period experies?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: If no action is taken by the second user and the 7-day period expires, the key will remain in the Active state and will not be deleted and you must restart the dual authorization process to delete the key . All key operations will still be allowed on the key. The key and its associated data will continue to be accessible and will not be moved to the Destroyed state. No further action can be taken to delete the key until the first user authorizes deletion again.

input.speaker: user
input.text: What could happen if I force a deletion on a key?

input.speaker: agent
input.text:
If you force a deletion on a key won't succeed if the key is protecting a registered IBM Cloud resource that's non-erasable due to a retention policy, which is a Write Once Read Many (WORM) policy set on the your IBM Cloud resource. You can verify whether a key is associated with a non-erasable resource by checking the 'preventKeyDeletion" field in the registration details for the key. Then, you must contact an account owner to remove the retention policy on each registered IBM Cloud resource that is associated with the key before you can delete the key. However, You must use the force option to delete a root key that is registered with another cloud resource.
Registrations are associations between root keys and other cloud resources, such as Cloud Object Storage (COS) buckets or Cloud Databases deployments.

input.speaker: agent
input.text: You always must a strategy for backing up your Block Storage for VPC volumes. Here are some considerations:Cloud Identity and Access Management (IAM) Evaluate your IAM access permissions to create and manage backups. Volumes Evaluate which volumes are most important to back up. You can create backups of boot and data volumes. A volume with numerous changes and a lengthy retention period requires more attention than a volume with moderate changes. Also, the cumulative size of all backups for a volume can't exceed 10 TB. Backup schedule Determine a backup schedule based on the type of volumes that you're backing up. For example, you might want to back up critical data that changes frequently more often than static data. Retention Determine a retention policy for backups in the backup plan. As subsequent backups are created, keep in mind that you incur costs for each backup that you retain.  You do your first backup  with a seed( a complete , a full backup)using  Delta Technology. With this technology, "full backups" are created at each session, but it saves enormous amounts of space on the vault and decreases the amount of time each subsequent backup takes to complete. Also . Administrators can set backups to follow an hourly, daily, weekly, or custom schedule that targets full systems, specific directories, or even individual files.

input.speaker: agent
input.text: If you have a significant amount of debt and the housing market is rising, delaying your house purchase another year you could well end up forking out extra money on the mortgage due to the rise in house prices. However, in order to roll debt into a home purchase, you need to have a sufficient down payment. However, if you have a lot of debt, it's unlikely that you have the necessary funds for a substantial down payment. Therefore, renting and paying off your debt first might be the better option.

input.speaker: user
input.text: tell me more about debt consolidation - is it a good idea?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: Debt consolidation involves combining multiple debts into a single loan, ideally with a lower interest rate. This can simplify your payments and potentially save you money on interest. Another good reasons for a consolidation loan is psychological. However, there are some potential pitfalls to consider. For example, if you take out a loan with a lower interest rate but a longer term, you may end up paying more in interest over the life of the loan. It's important to carefully evaluate your financial situation and consider all your options before deciding whether debt consolidation is right for you. If the interest rate is higher it is not a good idea. If you do decide to pursue debt consolidation, it's important to shop around for the best interest rates and terms. Local credit unions may be a good place to start, as they may be more willing to work with you than larger banks. Additionally, be sure to read the fine print and understand all the terms and conditions of any loan you are considering.

input.text:
VMware Solutions is a set of IBM Cloud offerings that facilitate deploying and managing VMware virtualized environments. To maximize your VMware Solutions experience, you can use the user documentation, REST API documentation reference, solution architectures, and solution guides. These resources will help you seamlessly extend your existing VMware® virtualized data center into the IBM Cloud or host cloud-native applications.
The solution supports various use cases, including capacity expansion and contraction in the cloud as needed, migration to the cloud, disaster recovery to the cloud, and backup into the cloud. With this solution, you can establish a dedicated cloud environment for development, testing, training, lab, or production purposes. IBM Cloud® for VMware Solutions also enables fully automated and rapid deployments of VMware vCenter Server® in the IBM Cloud. These offerings complement the on-premises infrastructure and allow existing and future workloads to run in the IBM Cloud without conversion, utilizing the same tools, skills, and processes employed on-premises. For more information, refer to the Virtualization for extending virtualized private cloud documentation.

input.speaker: user
input.text: Do I need scripts?

input.speaker: agent
input.text:
Yes. Custom scripts are extension points in the pipeline where adopters, teams, and users can provide scripts to run custom tasks needed for their continuous integration and deployment strategies. These custom scripts control the pipeline stages. You can configure the behavior of stages, script content, and the base image that runs the scripts using a configuration file (pipeline-config.yaml). The scripts and configuration for pipeline stages are loaded from a Git repository (repo), which can be either the application (app) repo (similar to .travis.yml or Jenkinsfile) or a custom repo.
When any of the custom scripts start, the complete URL of the custom script file, including the file name and the commit hash, is printed at the beginning of the pipeline logs as follows: "The custom script can be viewed using the following link: 'https://<URL>/blob/<commit hash>/.pipeline-config.yaml'". This improves traceability. The scripts are designed to create a new VPC and install the SAP (SAP NW 7.x release) solution and its dedicated DB SAP HANA box in one task flow. They create a Schematics workspace for you where you can view your script logs and the resources that are provisioned.

input.speaker: user
input.text: What about installation?

input.speaker: agent
input.text:
To download Red Hat OpenShift 4.7, follow these steps:
1. Go to the Red Hat OpenShift Infrastructure Providers page.
2. Download the installer.
3. Get the Pull Secret.
4. Download the Red Hat Enterprise Linux CoreOS (RHEL CoreOS) OVA image. You can use the following code to download the OVA:
   For example: `curl -O https://mirror.openshift.com/pub/openshift-v4/dependencies/rhcos/4.x/latest/rhcos-4.x.3-x86_64-vmware.x86_64.ova` (Replace https://mirror.openshift.com/pub/openshift-v4/dependencies/rhcos/4.x/latest/rhcos-4.x.3-x86_64-vmware.x86_64.ova with the actual download link)
5. Download the command-line tools if you want to run the commands from a desktop or outside the Bastion host.
For more information on installing the Red Hat OpenShift user provider infrastructure, refer to "Installing a cluster on vSphere with user-provisioned infrastructure.

input.text: How can I protect my personal information from identity theft

input.speaker: agent
input.text:
Here are some tips to protect your personal information from identity theft:
1. Do not respond to or click on any links within unsolicited emails or pop-up boxes. They can put your personal information in jeopardy.
2. Be cautious when using your debit card online. Consider using a credit card instead as they typically provide added protection against fraudulent charges.
3. Only download mobile applications from official app stores and check other users’ reviews, as well as the app’s privacy policies, before downloading.

input.speaker: user
input.text: What was its religion?

input.speaker: agent
input.text:
During the reign of Constantine in the Byzantine Empire, Christianity was not declared the exclusive state religion, but it received special favor from the emperor, who granted it generous privileges. Constantine also established the principle that emperors could not unilaterally decide matters of religious doctrine, and instead, they should convene general ecclesiastical councils for that purpose. His summoning of the Synod of Arles and the First Council of Nicaea demonstrated his commitment to the unity of the Church and asserted his authority as its leader.The ascendancy of Christianity was briefly challenged when Emperor Julian came to power in 361 and sought to revive polytheism throughout the empire. He was later known as "Julian the Apostate" by the Church. However, Julian's efforts were reversed when he died in battle in 363.
The foundation of the Byzantine Empire was based on the belief that it was a reflection of the Kingdom of Heaven on Earth. Just as God ruled in Heaven, the Emperor, created in His image, was expected to govern on Earth and uphold His commandments. The empire aspired to be universal and aimed to encompass all the peoples of the Earth. Ideally, all people would be members of the one true Christian Church, the Orthodox Church.

input.text: Constantine I reorganized the Roman Empire in the early 4th century, around 324-337 AD. This reorganization included establishing Constantinople as the new capital and legalizing Christianity. Constantine's changes marked a significant shift that set the stage for the development of the Byzantine Empire as a distinct entity from the classical Roman Empire. Constantine moved the seat of the Empire to Constantinople, which he founded as a second Rome on the site of Byzantium, a city strategically located on the trade routes between Europe and Asia and between the Mediterranean and the Black Sea. Constantine significantly changed the Empire's military, monetary, civil, and religious institutions.

input.text: There are different theories about why Rome fell. One theory suggests that the victorious legions, who acquired the vices of strangers and mercenaries during distant wars, first oppressed the republic's freedom and later violated the majesty of the purple. Emperors, concerned for their personal safety and public peace, resorted to corrupting the military discipline, ultimately leading to the military government's weakening and dissolution. This weakening was further exacerbated by the partial institutions of Constantine, ultimately leading to the Roman world being overrun by Barbarians. Historians are still analyzing the reasons for the loss of political control over a vast territory and the survival of the Eastern Roman Empire. Alexander Demandt listed 210 theories on why Rome fell, and new ideas continue to emerge. Gibbon's classic formulation of reasons for the Fall sparked ongoing controversy about the role of Christianity but also emphasized other causes of internal decline and external attacks on the Empire.

input.text: The Romans did not consistently use a specific system for numbering the years from the founding of their city. Different historians had different dates for the founding. Roman historians used the AUC system to measure the number of years between events rather than to indicate the date of a year. The Fasti Capitolini used an epoch of 752 BC, while modern historians have adopted the epoch of Varro, 753 BC. The AUC dating system was sometimes added in Renaissance manuscripts, giving the false impression that the Romans numbered their years. Censorinus stated that, in his time, the AUC year began with the Parilia, celebrated on 21 April, which was seen as the actual anniversary of the foundation of Rome. According to Roman myth, Rome was founded by Romulus, the first to dedicate spoils taken from an enemy ruler to the Temple of Jupiter Feretrius. input.speaker: agent
input.text: Modern historians refer to the Byzantine Empire as such to distinguish it from ancient Rome. This name comes from Byzantium, the original name of Constantinople, the capital city of the empire. The citizens of the empire continued to refer to their empire as the Roman Empire or Romania and to themselves as "Romans."

input.speaker: user
input.text: What was its religion?

input.speaker: agent
input.text:
During the reign of Constantine in the Byzantine Empire, Christianity was not declared the exclusive state religion, but it received special favor from the emperor, who granted it generous privileges. Constantine also established the principle that emperors could not unilaterally decide matters of religious doctrine, and instead, they should convene general ecclesiastical councils for that purpose. His summoning of the Synod of Arles and the First Council of Nicaea demonstrated his commitment to the unity of the Church and asserted his authority as its leader.The ascendancy of Christianity was briefly challenged when Emperor Julian came to power in 361 and sought to revive polytheism throughout the empire. He was later known as "Julian the Apostate" by the Church. However, Julian's efforts were reversed when he died in battle in 363.
The foundation of the Byzantine Empire was based on the belief that it was a reflection of the Kingdom of Heaven on Earth. Just as God ruled in Heaven, the Emperor, created in His image, was expected to govern on Earth and uphold His commandments. The empire aspired to be universal and aimed to encompass all the peoples of the Earth. Ideally, all people would be members of the one true Christian Church, the Orthodox Church.

input.speaker: user
input.text: When was reorganized?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: Constantine I reorganized the Roman Empire in the early 4th century, around 324-337 AD. This reorganization included establishing Constantinople as the new capital and legalizing Christianity. Constantine's changes marked a significant shift that set the stage for the development of the Byzantine Empire as a distinct entity from the classical Roman Empire. Constantine moved the seat of the Empire to Constantinople, which he founded as a second Rome on the site of Byzantium, a city strategically located on the trade routes between Europe and Asia and between the Mediterranean and the Black Sea. Constantine significantly changed the Empire's military, monetary, civil, and religious institutions.

input.text: The Romans did not consistently use a specific system for numbering the years from the founding of their city. Different historians had different dates for the founding. Roman historians used the AUC system to measure the number of years between events rather than to indicate the date of a year. The Fasti Capitolini used an epoch of 752 BC, while modern historians have adopted the epoch of Varro, 753 BC. The AUC dating system was sometimes added in Renaissance manuscripts, giving the false impression that the Romans numbered their years. Censorinus stated that, in his time, the AUC year began with the Parilia, celebrated on 21 April, which was seen as the actual anniversary of the foundation of Rome. According to Roman myth, Rome was founded by Romulus, the first to dedicate spoils taken from an enemy ruler to the Temple of Jupiter Feretrius. In subsequent wars, blood-stained armor and weaponry were gathered and placed in temples as a symbol of respect towards the enemies' deities and as a way to win their patronage.

input.text: E. E. Cummings (October 14, 1894 -- September 3, 1962) was an American poet, painter, essayist, author, and playwright. His first published work was a collection of poems titled Tulips and Chimneys (1923), which introduced the public to his characteristic eccentric use of grammar and punctuation. In addition to his poetry, Cummings also wrote children's books and novels, showcasing his versatility as a writer.

input.text: What are the steps involved to delete the CIS instance?

input.speaker: user
input.text: What are the steps involved to delete the CIS instance?

input.speaker: agent
input.text:
To delete a CIS instance, you must first delete all global load balancers, pools, and health checks. Then, delete the associated domain (zone). Go to the Overview page and click the trash can icon next to the domain name located in the Service Details section to start the deletion process.
If you are moving your domain to a different provider, be sure to migrate your DNS records and other configuration information to the new provider before activating the domain there. Activating the domain before migrating from CIS can cause your domain to change to a "Moved" state.
You can visit the IBM Cloud Internet Services for more information on how to delete the CIS instance. Delete the CIS instance if you created it specifically for this tutorial.

input.speaker: user
input.text: Do I need to import or recreate my DNS records in CIS?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: No, before you switch the traffic for your domain to CIS, it's important to import or recreate your DNS records in CIS. Although you can skip this step, it's crucial to ensure that your DNS records are correctly configured in CIS to avoid any parts of your website becoming inaccessible. Here are the command options: DNS_DOMAIN_ID: The ID of the DNS domain. Since CIS doesn't support email traffic by default, you need to set the PTR record to the location of your email server. You may need to reach out to your email provider for help. Import records - By default, a total of 3500 DNS records are allowed (imported and created on CIS).

input.speaker: user
input.text: Can I add a custom domain?

input.speaker: agent
input.text:
To add a custom domain to IBM Cloud Internet Services, follow these steps:
1. Go to the Overview page of your CIS instance.
2. Under the Domain section, click the plus (+) icon next to your domain.
3. choose the 'Connect domain' option in the revealed section.
4. Complete the process by setting up DNS records and delegating domain management as instructed in the provided document.
After adding a new domain, you can switch between your domains using the list menu in the Domain section to configure settings for each domain.
Work with your Domain Name Service (DNS) provider or IBM Cloud DNS to register your custom domain. If the apps you want Ingress to expose are in different namespaces in one cluster, register the custom domain as a wildcard domain, such as .custom_domain.net. Note that custom domains are required to use Ingress with private ALBs.

input.text: What is the transportation layer?

input.speaker: agent
input.text:
Mutual Transport Layer Security (mTLS) authentication ensures that traffic is secure and trusted in both directions between a client and server. This feature is exclusively available for customers at the Enterprise or Security plan level. 
When mTLS is configured, access is only granted to requests with a corresponding client certificate. Upon reaching the application, CIS requests the client certificate. If the client fails to present the certificate, the request is denied. Otherwise, the key exchange proceeds.
The Transport Layer Security (TLS) options allow you to control whether visitors can browse your website over a secure connection and, if they do, how IBM Cloud® Internet Services connects to your origin server. Mutual TLS is not enabled by default. It is an additional service that requires prior authorization and enablement.

input.text: Is privacy protected?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: Yes. Data privacy is upheld through the use of private data collections. For example, multiple wholesalers and a group of farmers can be connected to a single channel. If a farmer and a wholesaler want to conduct a private transaction, they can create a channel for this purpose. Alternatively, they can choose to establish a private data collection on the smart contract governing their business interactions to maintain privacy regarding sensitive aspects of the sale, such as the price, without the need to create an additional channel. This policy applies to EU-US and Swiss-US customers. If a government wants access to data held by IBM on behalf of a SaaS client, IBM expects that government to deal directly with that client. Data privacy and governance are of utmost importance. As a leader in providing a fully managed and globally distributable Database-as-a-Service, IBM® Cloudant® for IBM Cloud® allows customers to position data in any global IBM Cloud® or AWS region. By providing customers with high levels of data mobility to meet their local needs, IBM® and IBM Cloudant take data privacy and governance seriously. IBM Cloud Delivery Services does not use DLP monitoring. Access controls are implemented on all databases restricted to privileged users only.

If the apps you want Ingress to expose are in different namespaces in one cluster, register the custom domain as a wildcard domain, such as .custom_domain.net. Note that custom domains are required to use Ingress with private ALBs.

input.speaker: user
input.text: What is the transportation layer?

input.speaker: agent
input.text:
Mutual Transport Layer Security (mTLS) authentication ensures that traffic is secure and trusted in both directions between a client and server. This feature is exclusively available for customers at the Enterprise or Security plan level. 
When mTLS is configured, access is only granted to requests with a corresponding client certificate. Upon reaching the application, CIS requests the client certificate. If the client fails to present the certificate, the request is denied. Otherwise, the key exchange proceeds.
The Transport Layer Security (TLS) options allow you to control whether visitors can browse your website over a secure connection and, if they do, how IBM Cloud® Internet Services connects to your origin server. Mutual TLS is not enabled by default. It is an additional service that requires prior authorization and enablement.

input.speaker: user
input.text: Is privacy protected?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: Yes. Data privacy is upheld through the use of private data collections. For example, multiple wholesalers and a group of farmers can be connected to a single channel. If a farmer and a wholesaler want to conduct a private transaction, they can create a channel for this purpose. Alternatively, they can choose to establish a private data collection on the smart contract governing their business interactions to maintain privacy regarding sensitive aspects of the sale, such as the price, without the need to create an additional channel. This policy applies to EU-US and Swiss-US customers. If a government wants access to data held by IBM on behalf of a SaaS client, IBM expects that government to deal directly with that client. Data privacy and governance are of utmost importance. As a leader in providing a fully managed and globally distributable Database-as-a-Service, IBM® Cloudant® for IBM Cloud® allows customers to position data in any global IBM Cloud® or AWS region. By providing customers with high levels of data mobility to meet their local needs, IBM® and IBM Cloudant take data privacy and governance seriously. IBM Cloud Delivery Services does not use DLP monitoring. Access controls are implemented on all databases restricted to privileged users only.

input.speaker: user
input.text: Alternative means of salary for my employees

input.speaker: user
input.text: Alternative means of salary for my employees

input.speaker: agent
input.text: There are different ways to consider salary compensation besides the traditional gross salary. Total compensation includes salary and bonuses, as well as the cost or value of employer-paid healthcare, retirement, company cars, expense accounts, stock options, and other valuable goods or services. Additionally, the total employee cost is generally double the gross cash compensation (salary + bonuses) and includes employer-paid payroll taxes and practical costs like the cost of your computer, cubicle or office furniture, and the portion of utility costs that keep you well-lit, clean and comfortable. It's also important to consider the cost of living in the area where your employees live, as this can significantly impact the actual value of their salaries. For example, $100k in California may not go as far as $100k in other parts of the U.S. Finally, it's worth noting that salary pay does not directly translate to hourly wages, as salaried employees are compensated for a week of work, which may require more or less than 40 hours.

input.speaker: user
input.text: Do employers need to report their employee's salaries and withhold money for income tax purposes?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: Yes. In many countries, employers are required to report their employee's salaries and withhold a portion for income tax, which is commonly known as "pay as you earn," "withholding taxes," or taxing "at the source." Your employer uses the W4 form in the USA to determine how much to withhold from your wages. If you leave everything at default, your salary is your only taxable income, and you only benefit from default deductions. Your employer will calculate your tax withholding based on this information. When you start a new job, your employer will typically ask you to fill out a tax form such as the W-4 Employee's Withholding Allowance Certificate so they can withhold the correct federal income tax from your wages. If your employer doesn't do this, you are responsible for calculating and filing your income taxes, especially if you are self-employed, an independent contractor, or paid under the table. If your employer is reputable, they will provide you with additional information, usually in February. You must file your taxes correctly before the IRS tax deadline, generally April 15th for most people. Do I have to worry about money in my bank account? Unless you have a significant amount in your bank savings account earning interest, you won't have to pay any sort of tax on money in your bank account. If you do earn enough taxable interest, the bank will send you the proper forms to file your taxes.

input.speaker: user
input.text: In that case, could you please explain why I still have to pay income tax?

input.text: The number of allowances you can claim is calculated by dividing your total deductions by the personal exemptions for the tax year. In 2015, the standard deduction for a married couple filing jointly is $12,600, and each person gets a personal exemption of $4,000. If your deductions exceed the standard deduction, you can subtract property taxes from your income. For example, if your income is $95,000, after taking into account the standard deduction of $12,600 and $8,000 in exemptions, your taxable income would be just under $10,000, unless you have additional undisclosed income. The most common tax credit for most people is student loan interest. If you pay $500 in student loan interest, that sum is subtracted from your tax bill.

input.speaker: user
input.text: What about FICA?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: In the United States, nearly all workers must pay Social Security and Medicare taxes, also known as payroll taxes or FICA. It's important to understand that these taxes are not savings plans, and the money you pay is not explicitly allocated to an account in your name. Instead, it funds the benefits received by current retirees and beneficiaries. When you retire, your benefits will be financed by the contributions of the still-employed workers paying these taxes. It's worth noting that FICA taxes are separate from federal and state income taxes, and as a sole proprietor, you are responsible for both types of taxes. Furthermore, there's a distinction between FICA taxes when you are an employee and when you are self-employed. Typically, FICA taxes are divided equally between the employer and the employee, with each paying half. However, if you are self-employed, you are responsible for paying both halves. This is commonly known as the self-employment tax. You may be receiving benefits, but you are also still working, so you still have to pay the tax.

input.speaker: user
input.text: Investments?

input.speaker: agent
input.text:
If you receive dividends on an investment, those are taxed. When you exchange investments for cash, you will have to pay tax on that, disregarding capital losses, capital loss carryovers, AGI thresholds, and other special rules. For most investments, you get taxed on any gain in value in the investment or dividends paid by that investment. Usually, you don't take the tax hit until you sell the investment and realize the gain, with some exceptions for mutual funds. For bonds, CDs, and other cash-type investments, you have to pay taxes in the year they pay out the interest or dividend.
1) You can put money (up to a certain limit) in a traditional IRA and can subtract that amount from your income for tax calculation for the year you invest it. However, you are going to pay taxes on it when you take the money out at retirement. It really just delays the taxes.
2) If you put the money in a Roth IRA, you don't get a tax break now, but you don't have to pay any taxes on the money or the gains when you take it out at retirement.
3) The gains from some mutual funds can be tax exempt, but that just saves you from paying tax on the increase in value.
4) Don't fall for scams that try to use insurance policies as investments to avoid taxes. The fees are enormous, which usually makes them a rip-off.

For example, $100k in California may not go as far as $100k in other parts of the U.S. Finally, it's worth noting that salary pay does not directly translate to hourly wages, as salaried employees are compensated for a week of work, which may require more or less than 40 hours.

input.speaker: user
input.text: Do employers need to report their employee's salaries and withhold money for income tax purposes?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: Yes. In many countries, employers are required to report their employee's salaries and withhold a portion for income tax, which is commonly known as "pay as you earn," "withholding taxes," or taxing "at the source." Your employer uses the W4 form in the USA to determine how much to withhold from your wages. If you leave everything at default, your salary is your only taxable income, and you only benefit from default deductions. Your employer will calculate your tax withholding based on this information. When you start a new job, your employer will typically ask you to fill out a tax form such as the W-4 Employee's Withholding Allowance Certificate so they can withhold the correct federal income tax from your wages. If your employer doesn't do this, you are responsible for calculating and filing your income taxes, especially if you are self-employed, an independent contractor, or paid under the table. If your employer is reputable, they will provide you with additional information, usually in February. You must file your taxes correctly before the IRS tax deadline, generally April 15th for most people. Do I have to worry about money in my bank account? Unless you have a significant amount in your bank savings account earning interest, you won't have to pay any sort of tax on money in your bank account. If you do earn enough taxable interest, the bank will send you the proper forms to file your taxes.

input.speaker: user
input.text: In that case, could you please explain why I still have to pay income tax?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: In the U.S., most salaries are expressed as "gross salaries" before individual taxes are deducted. You must report all income from work, regardless of whether you save, spend, or keep it aside. This includes income from non-tax-advantaged accounts, as well as any gains or losses and dividends received. Gains and losses are realized when you actually sell, and is the difference between the price you bought for, and the price you sold for. Gains are taxed at the capital gains rate, either short-term or long-term depending on how long you owned the stock

input.speaker: user
input.text: Deductions

input.speaker: agent
input.text: The number of allowances you can claim is calculated by dividing your total deductions by the personal exemptions for the tax year. In 2015, the standard deduction for a married couple filing jointly is $12,600, and each person gets a personal exemption of $4,000. If your deductions exceed the standard deduction, you can subtract property taxes from your income. For example, if your income is $95,000, after taking into account the standard deduction of $12,600 and $8,000 in exemptions, your taxable income would be just under $10,000, unless you have additional undisclosed income. The most common tax credit for most people is student loan interest. If you pay $500 in student loan interest, that sum is subtracted from your tax bill.

input.speaker: user
input.text: What about FICA?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: In the United States, nearly all workers must pay Social Security and Medicare taxes, also known as payroll taxes or FICA. It's important to understand that these taxes are not savings plans, and the money you pay is not explicitly allocated to an account in your name. Instead, it funds the benefits received by current retirees and beneficiaries. When you retire, your benefits will be financed by the contributions of the still-employed workers paying these taxes. It's worth noting that FICA taxes are separate from federal and state income taxes, and as a sole proprietor, you are responsible for both types of taxes. Furthermore, there's a distinction between FICA taxes when you are an employee and when you are self-employed. Typically, FICA taxes are divided equally between the employer and the employee, with each paying half. However, if you are self-employed, you are responsible for paying both halves. This is commonly known as the self-employment tax. You may be receiving benefits, but you are also still working, so you still have to pay the tax.

input.speaker: user
input.text: Is personal income tax the same as corporate tax?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: No, corporations are taxed on their profits. Multinational corporations can report their earnings in any country where they operate, irrespective of where the actual sale occurs. It's difficult to pinpoint precisely where a company 'made its money.' Consequently, the US does not attempt to do so; instead, we tax them on their worldwide earnings, minus taxes paid in other countries.

input.speaker: user
input.text: Investments?

input.speaker: agent
input.text:
If you receive dividends on an investment, those are taxed. When you exchange investments for cash, you will have to pay tax on that, disregarding capital losses, capital loss carryovers, AGI thresholds, and other special rules. For most investments, you get taxed on any gain in value in the investment or dividends paid by that investment. Usually, you don't take the tax hit until you sell the investment and realize the gain, with some exceptions for mutual funds. For bonds, CDs, and other cash-type investments, you have to pay taxes in the year they pay out the interest or dividend.
1) You can put money (up to a certain limit) in a traditional IRA and can subtract that amount from your income for tax calculation for the year you invest it. However, you are going to pay taxes on it when you take the money out at retirement. It really just delays the taxes.
2) If you put the money in a Roth IRA, you don't get a tax break now, but you don't have to pay any taxes on the money or the gains when you take it out at retirement.
3) The gains from some mutual funds can be tax exempt, but that just saves you from paying tax on the increase in value.
4) Don't fall for scams that try to use insurance policies as investments to avoid taxes. The fees are enormous, which usually makes them a rip-off.

input.speaker: user
input.text: Are they the same all over the USA?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: No. Personal income tax rates and rules differ from state to state in the USA. Seven U.S. states currently don't have an income tax: Alaska, Florida, Nevada, South Dakota, Texas, Washington, and Wyoming; some only tax income derived in the state, and some tax worldwide income (like New York or California), similarly to the Federal income taxes. However, suppose you're living abroad (i.e., you're a citizen or resident of a foreign country and live there). In that case, you're not considered a resident by most states (check with your state for specific definitions) for most, if not all, the time of your residency abroad. In such a case, you don't pay state taxes; you only pay federal taxes.  You have to remember that foreign income exclusion doesn't apply to the income from your 401k, so you pay the taxes as if you're in the US.

input.text: what do I need for an eclipse?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: To safely view a solar eclipse, you will need solar viewing or "eclipse" glasses, which are much darker than regular sunglasses and block most of the Sun's light. It is also recommended to use special filters over the lenses of cameras, telescopes, or binoculars when looking at the Sun. You can also use an indirect viewing method, such as a pinhole projector, which projects an image of the Sun onto a nearby surface. It is safe to look at the Moon with unprotected eyes or through a telescope during all types and during all stages of a lunar eclipse.

input.speaker: user
input.text: how can I make the projector?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: To make a pinhole projector for viewing a solar eclipse, you will need two pieces of white card stock, aluminum foil, tape, and a pin, paper clip, or pencil. First, cut a 1-inch to 2-inch square or rectangular hole in the middle of one of the pieces of card stock. Tape a piece of aluminum foil over the hole. Then, flip over the paper and use your pin, paper clip, or pencil to poke a small hole in the aluminum foil. To make your projection a bit more defined, try putting the bottom piece of card stock in a shadowed area while you hold the other piece in the sunlight.

input.speaker: user
input.text: Is refinancing the same as getting a mortgage?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: No, they are not the same. A mortgage is a secured debt consisting of a promissory note and a security interest that provides the mortgage holder with a secured interest in the property. A mortgage is a loan backed by a property, as specific laws cover it due to its wide usage. Therefore, the bank is not an "investor" in your house; it is simply lending you money with the property as collateral. Thus, it does not receive any share of the profit. On the other hand, refinancing, or re-fi, occurs when a debtor takes out a new loan, usually with new bank, to pay off an old one. This can be done for various reasons, primarily because the new loan terms will result in a lower monthly payment.

input.text: What is the typical down payment amount for a mortgage?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: Ideally, a 20% down payment is suggested when buying a house. The idea behind this is that if someone can't save 1/5 of the cost of a house, they might not be responsible enough to ensure loan repayment. Additionally, by putting down 20%, you can avoid paying costly private mortgage insurance (PMI).

input.speaker: user
input.text: Which is more convenient: renting or buying

input.speaker: agent
input.text: Renting vs. buying: There are advantages to buying, such as that instead of money going towards rent, with a mortgage, some of the payment goes towards interest, but you are also building equity. Eventually, you'll pay off the mortgage and can live rent-free. Buying with a 20% down payment can give you a strong position in the market. If you have the financial ability to assume the risk of owning, you may be better off. On the other hand, renting provides flexibility to move with minimum hassle, and the landlord assumes the risk of owning the property. Buying is sometimes better than renting, even if you're not mobile; that depends on local market conditions. If you're investing the money reasonably, you may do as well as or better than a homebuyer, and your funds will be significantly more liquid.

input.text: where does the penobscot river start and end

input.speaker: agent
input.text: The Penobscot River originates from four branches in various lakes in north-central Maine. It specifically begins where the West Branch meets the East Branch at Medway. The river flows into the Atlantic Ocean at Penobscot Bay, and historically served as an important trade route from the Atlantic coast to interior Maine.

input.text: The West branch.

input.text: where does the penobscot river start and end

input.text: The Penobscot River originates from four branches in various lakes in north-central Maine. It specifically begins where the West Branch meets the East Branch at Medway. The river flows into the Atlantic Ocean at Penobscot Bay, and historically served as an important trade route from the Atlantic coast to interior Maine.

input.speaker: user
input.text: The West branch.

input.speaker: agent
input.text: The West Branch is home to many types of fish, such as landlocked salmon and brook trout. There are many deep pools within the river due to the many slate ledges, making the river ideal for fishing from shore, although there are still areas that are good for canoe fishing. The West Branch is a world-class landlocked salmon river, while the East Branch is known for its smallmouth bass fishery. In 2008, the Atlantic Salmon Commission opened the main stem of the river to catch and release fly fishing for Atlantic salmon.

input.text: The Amazon River is located in the America continent, also called the Americas, and is the largest river basin in the world, covering about 40% of South America with an area of approximately 7,050,000 square kilometers (2,722,020 sq mi). It flows from west to east, starting from Iquitos in Peru and continuing across Brazil to the Atlantic. The river runs mainly through Brazil and Peru and is part of the border between Colombia and Peru. input.speaker: agent
input.text: The Penobscot River originates from four branches in various lakes in north-central Maine. It specifically begins where the West Branch meets the East Branch at Medway. The river flows into the Atlantic Ocean at Penobscot Bay, and historically served as an important trade route from the Atlantic coast to interior Maine.

input.speaker: user
input.text: The West branch.

input.speaker: agent
input.text: The West Branch is home to many types of fish, such as landlocked salmon and brook trout. There are many deep pools within the river due to the many slate ledges, making the river ideal for fishing from shore, although there are still areas that are good for canoe fishing. The West Branch is a world-class landlocked salmon river, while the East Branch is known for its smallmouth bass fishery. In 2008, the Atlantic Salmon Commission opened the main stem of the river to catch and release fly fishing for Atlantic salmon.

input.speaker: user
input.text: Are there any other important river systems?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: Yes, Main has the Androscoggin River, a 178-mile river located in the U.S. states of Maine and New Hampshire in northern New England. It joins the Kennebec River at Merrymeeting Bay in Maine before flowing into the Gulf of Maine on the Atlantic Ocean. The river is a popular fishing destination, offering opportunities to catch brook, rainbow, brown trout, landlocked salmon, and smallmouth bass.

input.speaker: user
input.text: Is this the best river for fishing in the country?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: The "best" river for fishing depends on the specific type of fish being pursued and the angler's preferences. The Yellowstone River is considered one of the world's greatest trout streams. It is officially classed as a blue ribbon stream in Montana from the park to the confluence with the Boulder River east of Livingston and from the mouth of Rosebud Creek near Rosebud, Montana, to the North Dakota border.  The Snake River was once one of the most important rivers for the spawning of anadromous fish -- which are hatched in the headwaters of rivers, live in the ocean for most of their lives, and return to the river to spawn -- in the United States.

input.speaker: user
input.text: The Snake River is the longest river in the country.

input.speaker: agent
input.text: No. The Snake River is the thirteenth longest river in the United States. The longest river is the Mississippi River, the chief river of the second-largest drainage system on the North American continent, second only to the Hudson Bay drainage system. Using the longest stream as the source, Brower's Spring in Montana, the source of the Missouri River, would give it an incredible length of 3,710 miles, making it the fourth longest river in the world after the Nile, Amazon, and Yangtze. If we use the Largest Stream measurement, the Ohio River, and by extension the Allegheny River would be the source, and the Mississippi would begin in Pennsylvania.

input.text: Which is the largest river in America?

input.text: The Mississippi River is the chief river of the second-largest drainage system on the North American continent and is the longest river in the United States.

input.text: That was not my question. When I say "America," I'm referring to the continent, not the United States of America.

input.speaker: agent
input.text: The Amazon River is located in the America continent, also called the Americas, and is the largest river basin in the world, covering about 40% of South America with an area of approximately 7,050,000 square kilometers (2,722,020 sq mi). It flows from west to east, starting from Iquitos in Peru and continuing across Brazil to the Atlantic. The river runs mainly through Brazil and Peru and is part of the border between Colombia and Peru. input.text: What are the instructions to do it using Carthage?

input.text: You can install mobile SDK with Carthage as follows. Create a file in the project directory for your app and name it Cartfile. Carthage downloads and builds the SDK, creates a directory that is called Carthage in the project directory for your app, and puts an OpenWhisk.framework file inside Carthage/build/iOS. You must then add OpenWhisk.framework file to the embedded frameworks in your Xcode project.

input.speaker: user
input.text: My friend wants to put my name down for a house he's buying. What risks would I be taking?

input.speaker: user
input.text: My friend wants to put my name down for a house he's buying. What risks would I be taking?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: There are several risks you might be taking if your friend puts your name down for a house he's buying.  Firstly, if you are co-signing the mortgage, the risk is that you will owe the bank the principal amount of the mortgage if your friend is unable to pay. You could come out of it a lot poorer and with your credit rating wrecked. Secondly, if you are not on the deed of the house, you would not have any legal rights to the property. This means that your friend could sell the house without your consent and you would still be responsible for the mortgage.  Thirdly, if your friend's house is a second home, he may not qualify for mortgage assistance or relief, increasing the risk for you as a co-signer.    Lastly, anyone considering this approach to talk to a lawyer and be aware of the potential tax implications.
