input.speaker: user
input.text: Benefits of FHA loan?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: FHA is an insurance policy to the bank saying that if you default they will cover a high percentage of the loan. An FHA loan has several benefits. Firstly, it only requires a 3.5% down payment and allows for lower credit scores compared to traditional mortgages. This makes it a viable option for first-time homebuyers or those with limited credit history. Secondly, the PMI costs associated with FHA loans can be reduced by the size of the down payment. However, it's important to note that FHA loans require mortgage insurance premiums (MIP) for the life of the loan, which can increase the overall cost of the mortgage.

input.text: How to become a real estate agent and does it pay?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: To become a real estate agent, you would typically need to take a pre-licensing course from a certified provider within your state, pass a licensing exam, and then complete a certain number of hours of post-licensing education. As for whether it pays, real estate agents typically make a commission of 3% to 3.5% of the sale price of a property, which can vary depending on the location and the sale price. However, earnings can be unpredictable and depend on various factors such as the number of transactions and the sale prices of the properties. And they only get paid if the house sells.

input.speaker: user
input.text: Buying vs renting a home.

input.speaker: agent
input.text: The decision between buying and renting a home is a complex one that depends mostly on what makes economic sense. Pros of buying: - Building equity: When you buy a home, some of your monthly payment goes towards building equity in the property. - Tax benefits: Mortgage interest and property taxes are tax-deductible. - Freedom to customize: When you own a home, you have the freedom to customize it to your liking. - Stability: Owning a home can provide a sense of stability and security. Cons of buying: - Upfront costs: Buying a home typically requires a significant down payment and closing costs. - Maintenance and repairs: Homeowners are responsible for maintenance and repairs. A lot depends on how much of that you do yourself - Lack of mobility: buying only makes sense if you stay in the house for 5 years or more. Pros of renting: Rent. If you have no idea whether you will still be in the same part of the country five years from now - Lower upfront costs: Renting typically requires less upfront costs compared to buying. - No responsibility for maintenance and repairs. Cons of renting: - No equity building. - Rent increases: Rents can increase over time. - Limited customization: Renters typically have limited ability to customize their living space. Consider your individual needs, circumstances, and financial situation when making the decision between buying and renting.

While you can technically complete a real estate transaction without an agent, it's strongly advised to have one to guide you through the process and help you avoid potential pitfalls.

input.speaker: user
input.text: How to become a real estate agent and does it pay?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: To become a real estate agent, you would typically need to take a pre-licensing course from a certified provider within your state, pass a licensing exam, and then complete a certain number of hours of post-licensing education. As for whether it pays, real estate agents typically make a commission of 3% to 3.5% of the sale price of a property, which can vary depending on the location and the sale price. However, earnings can be unpredictable and depend on various factors such as the number of transactions and the sale prices of the properties. And they only get paid if the house sells.

input.speaker: user
input.text: Buying vs renting a home.

input.speaker: agent
input.text: The decision between buying and renting a home is a complex one that depends mostly on what makes economic sense. Pros of buying: - Building equity: When you buy a home, some of your monthly payment goes towards building equity in the property. - Tax benefits: Mortgage interest and property taxes are tax-deductible. - Freedom to customize: When you own a home, you have the freedom to customize it to your liking. - Stability: Owning a home can provide a sense of stability and security. Cons of buying: - Upfront costs: Buying a home typically requires a significant down payment and closing costs. - Maintenance and repairs: Homeowners are responsible for maintenance and repairs. A lot depends on how much of that you do yourself - Lack of mobility: buying only makes sense if you stay in the house for 5 years or more. Pros of renting: Rent. If you have no idea whether you will still be in the same part of the country five years from now - Lower upfront costs: Renting typically requires less upfront costs compared to buying. - No responsibility for maintenance and repairs. Cons of renting: - No equity building. - Rent increases: Rents can increase over time. - Limited customization: Renters typically have limited ability to customize their living space. Consider your individual needs, circumstances, and financial situation when making the decision between buying and renting.

input.speaker: user
input.text: Is a home a good investment?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: A home is the first and best investment where you build a life, but the value it returns isn't primarily.  It's not like a stock or bond that you hold for a little while and then cash out for the profit.  You can own a home with as little as 3% down and as you pay for your living you pay it off.   Others argue that it is not a good investment because it is a single, indivisible, undiversified, and illiquid asset, and its value is tied to the local economy, which can lead to losses during a recession and housing bust. It is an expense/liability/asset. Therefore, whether a home is a good investment or not depends on various factors and individual financial goals.

input.text: how to balance 401k?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: Balancing a 401k involves reallocating and rebalancing the investments within the account. You should determine the overall allocation between bonds, stocks, international, REIT, etc. This will depend on your risk tolerances and the total money in your 401k and IRA.  Once you figure out the allocation, map the available funds in your 401k according to this allocation. It is important to rebalance your 401k every year to maintain your desired asset allocation. Additionally you should review your allocation in a few years or after a life event such as a change of job, getting married, having kids, or buying a house. You can move from investment X to investment Y within your 401k without tax consequences.

input.speaker: user
input.text: How is 401k account taxed?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: 401(k) contributions are pre-tax dollars. A 401k account is taxed when you withdraw the money upon retirement. This is similar to how an IRA is taxed. The idea behind this is that you will likely be in a lower tax bracket at the time of retirement. It's important to note that 401k contributions reduce your taxable income for each paycheck, but they do not impact the level of your social security and medicare taxes. Also, the employer match is considered free money.

input.speaker: user
input.text: How can I compare the metrics for different periods?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: To compare the metrics for different periods, you can use the Time period control to select the desired time frames. You can choose from predefined periods such as a single day, a week, a month, or a quarter, or create custom time periods. The data points on the graph will adjust to an appropriate measurement period for the selected time frame. For example, when viewing a graph for a day, the data is presented in hourly values, but when viewing a graph for a week, the data is shown by day. Additionally, you can use the Refresh data option to ensure the data is up-to-date.

input.speaker: user
input.text: What are the different types of clouds?

input.speaker: agent
input.text:
There are three main types of clouds: public, private, and hybrid.
A public cloud consists of shared resources that provide access to a company's resources. It is hosted in a multi-tenant environment on a virtual server and can be accessed from anywhere.
A private cloud consists of resources that provide access to a company's resources. It is hosted on dedicated hardware, such as a bare metal server, either onsite at the company's office (or across offices) or by a cloud provider. A private cloud can be accessed from anywhere.
A hybrid cloud is a flexible solution that combines aspects of both public and private clouds. It is hosted both onsite at a company's office or across multiple offices, and by a cloud provider. This allows a hybrid cloud to be accessed from any location, making it an attractive option for many businesses.

The IBM Virtual Private Cloud (VPC) enables you to create a virtual private cloud by defining a virtual network isolated from other public cloud tenants. The software-defined networking (SDN) and virtual network functions allow you to set up the necessary network structures quickly and on-premises connectivity for running your workload. A VPC remains private by default, but it can be configured to enable public connectivity, such as through a public gateway or floating IP. With VPC, you can:
- Create a software-defined network (SDN)
- Isolate workloads
- Have fine control over inbound and outbound traffic

input.text: what about the goal of Deep Impact mission?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: The goal of the Deep Impact mission was to rendezvous with comet 9P/Tempel 1 and launch a projectile into the comet nucleus. Observations were then made of the ejecta, crater formation process, the resulting crater, and outgassing from the nucleus, particularly the newly exposed surface. The mission aimed to improve the knowledge of the physical characteristics of cometary nuclei and directly assess the interior of cometary nucleus, determine properties of the surface layers, study the relationship between the surface layers of a cometary nucleus and the possibly pristine materials of the interior, and improve our understanding of the evolution of cometary nuclei, particularly their approach to dormancy.

input.speaker: agent
input.text: The Deep Impact spacecraft consists of a 370 kg cylindrical copper impactor attached to a 650 kg flyby bus. The spacecraft is a box-shaped honeycomb aluminum framework with a flat rectangular Whipple debris shield mounted on one side. There are two high- and medium-resolution instruments, each consisting of an imaging camera and an infrared spectrometer. The medium resolution camera has a field of view of 0.587 degrees and a resolution of 7 m/pixel at 700 km distance, while the high resolution camera has a field of view of 0.118 degrees and a resolution of 1.4 m/pixel at 700 km. These instruments are used to observe the ejected ice and dust from the comet nucleus.

input.speaker: agent
input.text: The Deep Impact mission, launched in 2005, was designed to study comet 9P/Tempel 1. The spacecraft transferred into a heliocentric orbit and will rendezvous with comet P/Tempel 1 in July 2005.The spacecraft consisted of a flyby bus and a copper impactor, which were used to collide with the comet's nucleus and study the resulting ejecta and crater. The mission's scientific objectives included improving our understanding of the physical characteristics of cometary nuclei, determining the properties of the surface layers, studying the relationship between the surface and interior of the comet, and learning more about the evolution of cometary nuclei. After the primary mission, Deep Impact was extended to include the Deep Impact Extended Investigation (DIXI) and the Extrasolar Planet Observation and Characterization (EPOCh) investigations. DIXI involved flying by comet 103P/Hartley 2, while EPOCh used the Deep Impact imaging system to observe nearby bright stars with known large planets and search for other planets in those systems.

input.text: There are various types of dialog nodes. input.speaker: user
input.text: How is it created?

input.speaker: agent
input.text:
To create a dialogue, follow these steps:
1. Click the Dialog tab and then click Create dialog. When you open the dialog editor for the first time, the Welcome and Anything else nodes will be created for you.
2. To add more nodes, click the "More" icon on the Welcome node and select Add node below.
3. In the "If assistant recognizes" field, enter a condition that, when met, triggers your assistant to process the node. Typically, you want to add an intent as the condition.
4. Specify the condition and response for each node. The condition specifies the information that must be present in the user input for the node to be triggered, while the response is the utterance that your assistant uses to respond to the user.
5. Save your progress.
Remember, the dialogue matches intents (what users say) to responses (what the bot says back) and can ask for more details in a text response and create one or more child nodes to process the new input.

Remember, the dialogue matches intents (what users say) to responses (what the bot says back) and can ask for more details in a text response and create one or more child nodes to process the new input.

input.text: Do common stocks and preferred stocks have any differences in terms of percentage of the company per unit they represent?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: Yes, common stocks and preferred stocks have differences in terms of what they represent per unit. Common stocks typically represent ownership in a company and usually come with voting rights. On the other hand, preferred stocks usually do not come with voting rights but offer more consistent dividends and have a higher claim on assets and earnings than common stocks in case of liquidation. However, neither common nor preferred stocks necessarily represent a specific percentage of the company per unit, as the value of each share can vary based on factors such as the company's performance and market conditions.

input.speaker: user
input.text: Tax-wise, how does it work?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: Purchasing stock doesn't affect your immediate taxes, however, selling stock has tax effects; if you sell the stocks for a profit, you will have a capital gain, which is subject to capital gains tax. If you sell them for a loss, you can use that to offset other capital gains or potentially deduct a certain amount from your taxable income.  It would be beneficial to consult with a tax professional or financial advisor to understand the tax implications better. Regarding the "zero dividend preference shares," they are designed to deliver gains as capital gains rather than income. This feature can be useful for tax efficiency, especially when making use of the annual capital gains tax allowance. However, these types of shares may have gone out of fashion due to a scandal around the year 2000.

input.text: Yes, there are provisions for losses in stock trading. If you have incurred a loss, you can take advantage of it through tax loss harvesting. This involves selling the stock at a loss and then buying it back after a 30-day waiting period. The loss can be used to offset other capital gains or potentially deduct a certain amount from your taxable income. It's important to note that there are rules and limitations to this strategy, such as the wash sale rule, which prohibits repurchasing the same or a substantially identical security within 30 days before or after the sale.

input.text: FIFO or LIFO?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: The choice between the First-In-First-Out (FIFO) and Last-In-First-Out (LIFO) methods for determining which shares are sold first when you sell shares of a stock you own can have different tax implications. Under the FIFO method, the oldest shares are considered sold first and pay lower capital gains tax rate instead of the short term marginal rate.  Under the LIFO method, the most recently acquired shares are sold first.    If you're selling at loss and have other short term gains, you would probably be better selling LIFO, so that the loss could offset other short term gains that you might have.  The default is FIFO.

input.speaker: user
input.text: What size should the bin be for compost?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: In Madison County, NY., the bin for composting should be at least 3x3x3 feet in size.

input.speaker: user
input.text: can you please explain the steps for composting at home?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: Sure, I'd be happy to explain the steps for composting at home as outlined by Madison County, NY. Step 1: Pick Out A Bin - The bin should be at least 3x3x3 feet in size. The location can affect how fast your compost dries out or holds water. A closed bin is recommended to keep critters out. If you use a closed bin, add a shovel full of soil from the ground. Keep your bin somewhere convenient to use and access. Step 2: Gather Materials - Browns are higher in carbon, and greens are higher in nitrogen. The optimal ratio is 3 parts brown to 1 part green. Avoid feces from cats and dogs, invasive plants, weed seeds, oil, fats, plastics, or non-organic matter. Gather kitchen waste in a container by your sink. Step 3: Maintain - If you have an open bin, layer some small sticks and twigs at the bottom to help with air and drainage. Layer your browns and greens, imagining you're making a bowl for your greens to lay in the middle and then adding more browns on top. Keep your compost at 40-60% water content, like a wrung out sponge. Turning helps a pile break down faster and ensures oxygen is present. Step 4: Harvest and More! - You will know when your compost is done when it has an earthy smell and looks like rich dark soil. If you do not fully turn the pile, your finished compost will be at the bottom of the pile. If you're running out of browns, shredded newspaper, paper towel, and toilet paper rolls make good brown additions. For more resources, you can check out www.compost.css.cornell.edu, www.epa.gov/recycle/composting-home, and www.dec.ny.gov/

input.speaker: agent
input.text: The optimal temperature range for beneficial microorganisms in a compost pile is between 131 to 160 degrees Fahrenheit. This temperature range promotes rapid decomposition and destroys pathogens and weed seeds. Pile temperatures must remain at 131 degrees Fahrenheit or above for several days to meet standards for reducing pathogens. The exact amount of time depends on the method of composting. Controlling the oxygen, moisture, pile structure, and nutrient balance helps achieve the optimal temperature range in a compost pile. The ideal temperature for a vermicomposting system is 55 to 80 degrees Fahrenheit.

input.text: What size should the bin be for compost?

input.text: In Madison County, NY., the bin for composting should be at least 3x3x3 feet in size.

input.speaker: user
input.text: can you please explain the steps for composting at home?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: Sure, I'd be happy to explain the steps for composting at home as outlined by Madison County, NY. Step 1: Pick Out A Bin - The bin should be at least 3x3x3 feet in size. The location can affect how fast your compost dries out or holds water. A closed bin is recommended to keep critters out. If you use a closed bin, add a shovel full of soil from the ground. Keep your bin somewhere convenient to use and access. Step 2: Gather Materials - Browns are higher in carbon, and greens are higher in nitrogen. The optimal ratio is 3 parts brown to 1 part green. Avoid feces from cats and dogs, invasive plants, weed seeds, oil, fats, plastics, or non-organic matter. Gather kitchen waste in a container by your sink. Step 3: Maintain - If you have an open bin, layer some small sticks and twigs at the bottom to help with air and drainage. Layer your browns and greens, imagining you're making a bowl for your greens to lay in the middle and then adding more browns on top. Keep your compost at 40-60% water content, like a wrung out sponge. Turning helps a pile break down faster and ensures oxygen is present. Step 4: Harvest and More! - You will know when your compost is done when it has an earthy smell and looks like rich dark soil. If you do not fully turn the pile, your finished compost will be at the bottom of the pile. If you're running out of browns, shredded newspaper, paper towel, and toilet paper rolls make good brown additions. For more resources, you can check out www.compost.css.cornell.edu, www.epa.gov/recycle/composting-home, and www.dec.ny.gov/

input.speaker: user
input.text: Does he understand my emotions?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: Yes, Watson Assistant has the capability to understand human emotions to some extent. However, this is not through some mystical communion with cosmic energy, but through features like Emotion Analysis in certain IBM Watson products.  It can evaluates the overall emotional tone like joy, disgust, anger, fear, and sadness in a given text. It calculates a score for each emotion on a scale of 0.0 to 1.0, and if the score of any emotion is 0.5 or higher, it is considered detected, currently though in English only.  However, it does not understand or respond to human emotions directly. It analyzes text to determine the presence and intensity of certain emotions, but it cannot perceive or react to the emotions of individual users.

input.text: where does water go after it enters a storm drain

input.speaker: user
input.text: where does water go after it enters a storm drain

input.speaker: agent
input.text: The water that enters a storm drain like some of the heavier sediment and small objects may settle in a catchbasin , or sump , which lies immediately below the outlet , where water from the top of the catchbasin reservoir overflows into the sewer proper . Most drains have a single large exit at their point of discharge ( often covered by a grating ) into a canal , river , lake , reservoir , sea or ocean . Storm drains may discharge into man - made excavations known as recharge basins or retention ponds .

input.speaker: user
input.text: What is a catchbasin and how they are designed?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: A catchbasin is a component in a storm drain system that serves as a first-line pretreatment for other treatment practices. It functions similarly to the trap in household wastewater plumbing by capturing large sediments and street litter from urban runoff before it enters the storm drainage pipes. It is designed with a single large exit at its point of discharge, often covered by a grating, and lies immediately below the outlet of the storm drain. Water from the top of the catchbasin reservoir overflows into the sewer proper, and most catchbasins are connected to a piping system that leads to a canal, river, lake, reservoir, sea, or ocean.  Unlike the plumbing trap , the catchbasin does not necessarily prevent sewer gases such as hydrogen sulfide and methane from escaping. Its performance at removing sediment and other pollutants depends on the design of the catchbasin ( for example , the size of the sump ) , and on routine maintenance to retain the storage available in the sump to capture sediment . Some  catch basins are also commonly designed with a sump area below the outlet pipe level which is a reservoir for water and debris to help prevent the pipe from clogging

input.text: At the federal level in the United States,  we have the National Environmental Policy Act ( NEPA ) and the Environmental Protection Agency ( EPA )created and signed during President Nixon term. Later solid waste contaminants from agriculture , industry , mining , municipalities , and others were put under control .EPA have spent more than than $8.7 million on environmental education annually to increase public knowledge and awareness about the current environmental issues and the consequences . They aim to promote the popularization of the necessary skills and knowledge for the public to make decisions and participate in activities that lead to the resolution of environmental challenges . Virtually all of the executive branch 's departments have some area of environmental authority .The Environmental Protection Agency ( EPA ) issued stormwater regulations for  cities , suburban communities and towns with over 10,000 population requiring them  to obtain discharge permits for their storm sewer systems , under the Clean Water Act . The permits require local governments to operate stormwater management programs , covering both construction of new buildings and facilities , and maintenance of their existing municipal drainage networks . State government facilities , such as roads and highways , are also subject to the stormwater management regulations . input.text: I do not have much information about how storm drain works around the world, but an international subculture has grown up around the exploration of stormwater drains . Storm drain , storm sewer ( U.S. and Canada ) , surface water drain / sewer ( United Kingdom ) , or stormwater drain ( Australia and New Zealand ) is usually designed to drain excess rain and ground water from impervious surfaces such as paved streets , car parks , parking lots , footpaths , sidewalks , and roofs. In UK, there are here are two main types of stormwater drain : the highway drain or road gully in the UK inlets.

input.text: As I mentioned before the are commonly designed to capture large sediments and street litter from urban runoff before it enters the storm drainage pipes. However its  functionality is questionable , as these catch basins are most commonly not cleaned annually as is needed to make them perform as designed . The trapping of debris serves no purpose because once filled they operate as if no basins were present , but continue to allow a shallow area of water retention for the breeding of mosquito since it is cool , dark , and retains stagnant water for long periods of time . Combined with standard grates which have holes large enough for mosquitoes to enter and leave the basin , this is a major problem in mosquito control .  . Moreover , even if cleaned and maintained , the water reservoir remains filled , accommodating the breeding of mosquitoes .

input.text: Could be the water that enters a storm drain recycled and be used in the house chores like cleaning, gardening , etc?

input.text: The water from urban runoff is very dirty. It tends to pick up gasoline , motor oil , heavy metals , trash and other pollutants from roadways and parking lots , as well as fertilizers and pesticides from lawns  and may become contaminated while running down the road or other impervious surface , before entering the drain and could not be used in house chores. However,  Many local governments conduct public awareness campaigns about this including sustainable urban drainage systems or low impact development or green infrastructure practices   into municipal plans , helping lest waste be dumped into the storm drain system . In the city of Cleveland , Ohio , for example , all new catch basins installed have inscriptions on them not to dump any waste , and usually include a fish imprint as well .

input.speaker: user
input.text: And about on the level of the Federal government, do  they have guidelines or even a law to how to build more sustainable storm drain so the water could be recycled ?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: At the federal level in the United States,  we have the National Environmental Policy Act ( NEPA ) and the Environmental Protection Agency ( EPA )created and signed during President Nixon term. That legislation was concerned primarily first - generation pollutants in the air , surface water , groundwater , and solid waste disposal . Later solid waste contaminants from agriculture , industry , mining , municipalities , and others were put under control .EPA have spent more than than $8.7 million on environmental education annually to increase public knowledge and awareness about the current environmental issues and the consequences . They aim to promote the popularization of the necessary skills and knowledge for the public to make decisions and participate in activities that lead to the resolution of environmental challenges . Virtually all of the executive branch 's departments have some area of environmental authority .The Environmental Protection Agency ( EPA ) issued stormwater regulations for  cities , suburban communities and towns with over 10,000 population requiring them  to obtain discharge permits for their storm sewer systems , under the Clean Water Act . The permits require local governments to operate stormwater management programs , covering both construction of new buildings and facilities , and maintenance of their existing municipal drainage networks . Many municipalities have revised their local ordinances covering management of runoff . State government facilities , such as roads and highways , are also subject to the stormwater management regulations . Many local municipalities have placed ordinances for both commercial and residential stormwater management practices to be designed , implemented , and approved before an occupancy permit is released .New developments might be required to construct their own storm drain processing capacity for returning the runoff to the water table and bioswales may be required in sensitive ecological areas to protect the watershed

Storm drain , storm sewer ( U.S. and Canada ) , surface water drain / sewer ( United Kingdom ) , or stormwater drain ( Australia and New Zealand ) is usually designed to drain excess rain and ground water from impervious surfaces such as paved streets , car parks , parking lots , footpaths , sidewalks , and roofs. In UK, there are here are two main types of stormwater drain : the highway drain or road gully in the UK inlets.

input.speaker: user
input.text: Do you have any information about how the ancient civilization deal with the stormwater?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: The storm drain Archaeological studies have revealed the use of quite sophisticated stormwater runoff systems in ancient cultures . For instance , in Minoan Crete approximately 4000 years before present , cities such as Phaistos were designed to have storm drains and channels to collect precipitation runoff . At Cretan Knossos , storm drains include stone - lined structures large enough for a person to crawl through . Other examples of early civilizations with elements of stormwater drain systems include early people of Mainland Orkney such as Gurness and the Brough of Birsay in Scotland . Even the success of ancient Egyptian civilization came partly from its ability to use and adapt the stormrain flooding of the Nile River valley for agriculture .

input.speaker: user
input.text: where does water go after it enters a storm drain

input.speaker: agent
input.text: The water that enters a storm drain like some of the heavier sediment and small objects may settle in a catchbasin , or sump , which lies immediately below the outlet , where water from the top of the catchbasin reservoir overflows into the sewer proper . Most drains have a single large exit at their point of discharge ( often covered by a grating ) into a canal , river , lake , reservoir , sea or ocean . Storm drains may discharge into man - made excavations known as recharge basins or retention ponds .

input.speaker: user
input.text: What is a catchbasin and how they are designed?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: A catchbasin is a component in a storm drain system that serves as a first-line pretreatment for other treatment practices. It functions similarly to the trap in household wastewater plumbing by capturing large sediments and street litter from urban runoff before it enters the storm drainage pipes. It is designed with a single large exit at its point of discharge, often covered by a grating, and lies immediately below the outlet of the storm drain. Water from the top of the catchbasin reservoir overflows into the sewer proper, and most catchbasins are connected to a piping system that leads to a canal, river, lake, reservoir, sea, or ocean.  Unlike the plumbing trap , the catchbasin does not necessarily prevent sewer gases such as hydrogen sulfide and methane from escaping. Its performance at removing sediment and other pollutants depends on the design of the catchbasin ( for example , the size of the sump ) , and on routine maintenance to retain the storage available in the sump to capture sediment . Some  catch basins are also commonly designed with a sump area below the outlet pipe level which is a reservoir for water and debris to help prevent the pipe from clogging

input.text: Chaging the subject a little a bit,  are there many people worldwide without  proper sewage treatment?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: Unfortunately there are many countries worldwide with environmental issues  and theirs citizens living without a  proper sewage treatment or even having access to clean water. For instance, in Philippines only 5 % of the total population is connected to a sewer network . The vast majority uses flush toilets connected to septic tanks . Since sludge treatment and disposal facilities are rare , most effluents are discharged without treatment . Same problem in India where,  according to a World Health Organization study , out of country 's 3,119 towns and cities , just 209 have partial sewage treatment facilities , and only 8 have full wastewater treatment facilities . Over 100 Indian cities dump untreated sewage directly into the Ganges River . In Venezuela, 13 % of the population lacked access to drinking water , but this number had been dropping . Surprisingly  in the United States  more than 1.6 million people in 630,000 households , still lack basic plumbing facilities . More than a third of them have household incomes below the federal poverty level . They are spread across all racial and ethnic categories , but they are more prominent in the minority groups . Most of the people who lacked plumbing services were elderly , poor , and living in rural areas . Alaska has the highest percentage of households without plumbing -- 6.32 percent of all its households . One in eight people in the world do not having access to safe water inappropriate use of water may contribute to this  situation .

input.text: How can I create one?

input.speaker: agent
input.text:
To sign up for an IBM Cloud account, you can use an existing IBMid or create a new one. Simply go to the IBM Cloud login page and click on "Create an IBM Cloud account." Then, enter your IBMid email address. If you don't have an existing IBMid, an ID will be created based on the email you enter. Complete the remaining fields with your information.
You will be prompted to provide your credit card information to verify your identity and secure your account. It's important to note that you can try out IBM Cloud for free and only pay for the billable services that you choose to use, with no long-term contracts or commitments. After filling out the information, click "Create account" and then confirm your account by clicking the link in the confirmation email sent to your provided email address.
Finally, you can see the "Account types" section to compare and choose an account type that suits your needs.

input.speaker: user
input.text: What are the advantages and disadvantages of Immutable data Storage?

input.speaker: user
input.text: What are the advantages and disadvantages of Immutable data Storage?

input.speaker: agent
input.text:
There are some potential advantages and disadvantages of Immutable Object Storage:  
Advantages:  
1. Data Integrity and Long-term Data Retention: Immutable Object Storage preserves electronic records and maintains data integrity. It ensures that data is stored in a WORM (Write-Once-Read-Many), no-erasable and non-rewritable manner, which can be beneficial for industries like Financial, Healthcare, and Media content archives where data integrity is crucial. 
 2. Compliance: Immutable Object Storage can help organizations comply with certain regulations like SEC Rule 17a-4(f), FINRA Rule 4511(c), and CFTC Rule 1.31(c)-(d) that govern electronic records storage and retention. When using immutable Object Storage, it is the client's responsibility to check for and ensure whether any of the feature capabilities that are discussed can be used to satisfy and comply with the key rules around electronic records storage and retention governed by the above-mentioned regulations.
Disadvantages:  
1. Limited Availability: Immutable Object Storage is available in certain regions only. Users outside these regions may not be able to use this feature. 
2. Pricing: It requires a Standard pricing plan, which could be a disadvantage for users looking for more affordable options.  
3. No High-speed Transfer: It isn't possible to use Aspera high-speed transfer with buckets with a retention policy. This could potentially slow down the data transfer process.  
4. Irreversible: Once permanent retention is enabled, it can't be reversed. Objects uploaded that use a permanent retention period cannot be deleted. This means users need to be sure about their need to permanently store objects. 
5. Legal Holds: The retention policy is enforced until the end of a retention period and the removal of any legal holds. This could potentially cause issues if there are legal disputes or investigations. Please note that the actual advantages and disadvantages may vary depending on the specific use case and requirements.
To assist clients in making informed decisions, IBM engaged Cohasset Associates Inc. to conduct an independent assessment of IBM’s Immutable Object Storage.

input.text: The Great Wall of China was built using various materials over different periods. Originally, it was mainly constructed from rammed earth, stones, and wood. input.text: How long is the Wall?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: The frontier walls built by different dynasties have multiple courses. Collectively, they stretch from Dandong in the east to Lop Lake in the west, from present - day Sino - Russian border in the north to Qinghai in the south. The walls built by the Ming dynasty measure 8,850 km. Faced with the threat of the Göktürks from the north, the Qi built up to 3,000 li about 1,600 kilometers. The Xuan - Da portion of the Great Wall totaled about 850 kilometers of wall, with some sections being doubled - up with two lines of wall, some tripled or even quadrupled. Chancellor Wanyan Xiang convinced the emperor to build an extensive systems of walls, which consisted of a 700 kilometers outer wall from Heilongjiang to Mongolia and a 1,000 kilometers network of inner walls, north and northeast of Beijing. Together, they formed a roughly elliptical web of fortifications 1,400 kilometers in length.

input.speaker: user
input.text: Who was the enemy?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: Many scholars have operated under the belief that the Great Wall continually defended China 's border against the raids and invasions of the various nomadic groups of the Eurasian Steppe with an eye to expansion. Towards the end of the Ming, the Great Wall helped defend the empire against the Manchu invasions that began around 1600. The usefulness of the Great Wall as a defense line against northern nomads became questionable under the Qing dynasty, since China proper, Manchuria, and Mongolia were all under Qing control. So instead, the Great Wall became the means to limit Han Chinese movement into the steppes. Rather than being defensive, the walls indicate the northward expansions of the three northern states and their desire to safeguard their recent territorial acquisitions.

input.text: What is Qin Shi Huang known for?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: In 221 BC , the state of Qin completed its conquest over the other Warring States and united China under Qin Shi Huang , the first emperor of China. Intending to impose centralized rule and prevent the resurgence of feudal lords , he ordered the destruction of the sections of the walls that divided his empire. Contrary to the building of the outer walls, which were built to stabilize the newly united China, the inner walls threatened the unity of the empire. In the north, the state of Wei and the Yiqu built walls to protect themselves from Qin aggression, but were still unable to stop Qin from eating into their territories. The settlement of the north continued up to Qin Shi Huang 's death in 210 BC.

input.speaker: user
input.text: What did Meng Tian do?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: Meng Tian was a Chinese general and politician who served under Emperor Qin Shi Huang during the Qin Dynasty. He is best known for leading the northern expedition against the Xiongnu people. Meng Tian was responsible for building and extending the Great Wall of China along the empire's northern border. Meng Tian introduced 30,000 settler families to colonize the newly conquered territories. When Meng Tian's settlements in the north were abandoned, the Xiongnu nomads moved back into the Ordos Loop as the Qin empire became consumed by widespread rebellion. Upon Qin Shi Huang's death in 210 BC, Meng Tian was ordered to commit suicide in a succession conspiracy.

input.text: Is the Great Wall of China a tourist destination today?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: When China opened its borders to foreign merchants and visitors after its defeat in the First and Second Opium Wars, the Great Wall became a main attraction for tourists. The sections of the Great Wall around Beijing municipality are especially famous: they were frequently renovated and are regularly visited by tourists today. In 1952, the scholar - turned - bureaucrat Guo Moruo laid out the first modern proposal to repair the Great Wall. Five years later, the renovated Badaling became the first section to be opened to the public since the establishment of the PRC. The Badaling Great Wall has since become a staple stop for foreign dignitaries.

input.text: Where can people report a scam that they have been affected by?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: To report a scam, you can report the incident to TIGTA and to phishing@irs.gov (Subject: FAX). Additionally, if you have been a victim of monetary or identity theft, you may also file a complaint with the Internet Crime Complaint Center. If you are a U.S. citizen located in the United States or its territories or a U.S. citizen living abroad: complete the appropriate complaint form with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission. Forward email to phishing@irs.gov (Subject: Stock). If you are not a U.S. citizen and reside outside the United States, complete the appropriate complaint form with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission. Contact your securities regulator and file a complaint. Forward email to phishing@irs.gov (Subject: Stock).

input.speaker: user
input.text: How should I avoid to be a  victim of spam texts and emails?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: The best way to avoid being a victim of spam texts and emails, it is  never share your personal information, especially your Social Security number, with someone who reaches out to you unexpectedly. Unsubscribe from unwanted emails to reduce the chances of clicking on links that could lead to a phishing attack. Also report unwanted messages using your phone's "report junk" option or forwarding them to 7726 (SPAM) for texts and to your email provider for unwanted emails. Educate yourself by  learning more about how to get fewer spam texts and emails.  If you spot a scam, report it to the FTC at ReportFraud.ftc.gov. Additionally, before entering personal information on a website, email, or text chain, stop and ask yourself why they need this information and what's going to happen to it. Always be cautious when dealing with unsolicited messages.

input.speaker: user
input.text: Speaking about scams, what are currently the most common scams and frauds?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: Lottery and sweepstakes scams are one of the most common consumer frauds operating today.  You get a card, call, or email telling you that you won. Then  they tell you there is a fee, some taxes, or customs duties to pay. They ask for your bank account information, or ask you to send money via a wire transfer or to purchase gift cards and provide the card numbers. Government Impostor scams  are also very common : You get a text, call, or email from someone who says they are with the government. They may claim to be a U.S. Marshal, saying you must pay a fine for missing jury duty. Or the IRS, saying that you owe thousands in back taxes. Another scam that you must be alert occurs  when someone gets an email or most often a phone call and the caller begins speaking very quickly about common computer issues that slow performance or even potential hacks to your system. There are imposter scammers  that try to convince you to send money by pretending to be someone you know or trust like a sheriff, local, state, or federal government employee, or charity organization. Today, phishing  is very common . the scammer  impersonates a business or a person to trick you into giving out your personal information, such as passwords, credit card numbers, or bank account information. A scammer may use fraudulent emails, texts, or websites to steal this information from you.

input.text: Speaking about scams, what are currently the most common scams and frauds?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: Lottery and sweepstakes scams are one of the most common consumer frauds operating today.  You get a card, call, or email telling you that you won. Then  they tell you there is a fee, some taxes, or customs duties to pay. They ask for your bank account information, or ask you to send money via a wire transfer or to purchase gift cards and provide the card numbers. Government Impostor scams  are also very common : You get a text, call, or email from someone who says they are with the government. They may claim to be a U.S. Marshal, saying you must pay a fine for missing jury duty. Or the IRS, saying that you owe thousands in back taxes. Another scam that you must be alert occurs  when someone gets an email or most often a phone call and the caller begins speaking very quickly about common computer issues that slow performance or even potential hacks to your system. There are imposter scammers  that try to convince you to send money by pretending to be someone you know or trust like a sheriff, local, state, or federal government employee, or charity organization. Today, phishing  is very common . the scammer  impersonates a business or a person to trick you into giving out your personal information, such as passwords, credit card numbers, or bank account information. A scammer may use fraudulent emails, texts, or websites to steal this information from you.

input.text: To report a scam, you can report the incident to TIGTA and to phishing@irs.gov (Subject: FAX). input.text: How should I avoid to be a  victim of spam texts and emails?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: The best way to avoid being a victim of spam texts and emails, it is  never share your personal information, especially your Social Security number, with someone who reaches out to you unexpectedly. Unsubscribe from unwanted emails to reduce the chances of clicking on links that could lead to a phishing attack. Also report unwanted messages using your phone's "report junk" option or forwarding them to 7726 (SPAM) for texts and to your email provider for unwanted emails. Educate yourself by  learning more about how to get fewer spam texts and emails.  If you spot a scam, report it to the FTC at ReportFraud.ftc.gov. Additionally, before entering personal information on a website, email, or text chain, stop and ask yourself why they need this information and what's going to happen to it. Always be cautious when dealing with unsolicited messages.

input.speaker: user
input.text: do I have to have Boating safety certificates ?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: In New York, anyone operating a Personal Watercraft (JetSki™, Wave Runner™, etc.) must have a boating safety certificate and be at least 14 years of age or older. Starting in 2024, if you are operating any other type of motorized boat, you will need a safety certificate if you were born on or after January 1, 1978. Starting in 2025, all operators of motorized vessels, regardless of age, will be required to have a safety certificate. However, if you are renting a boat, you do not need a safety certificate if you are at least 18 years old, although the livery must demonstrate how to properly use the boat before renting it. Persons under the age of 18 may only rent a motorboat if they hold a safety certificate.

input.text: The Defense of Marriage Act (DOMA) is a United States federal law that defines and protects the institution of marriage. enacted in 1996. It was introduced by Georgia Representative Bob Barr and Senator Don Nickles of Oklahoma. The Act was intended by Congress to reflect and honor a collective moral judgment and to express moral disapproval of homosexuality. It also defined marriage for federal purposes as the legal union of a man and a woman as husband and wife, and a spouse as a husband or wife of the opposite sex. Section 3 of DOMA defines marriage and spouse for federal purposes. Section 3 codified non-recognition of same - sex marriages for all federal purposes, including insurance benefits for government employees, social security survivors ' benefits, immigration, bankruptcy, and the filing of joint tax returns, as well as excluding same - sex spouses from the scope of laws protecting families of federal officers, laws evaluating financial aid eligibility, and federal ethics laws applicable to opposite - sex spouses.

input.speaker: user
input.text: How are weddings celebrated?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: The typical wedding involves a couple proclaiming their commitment to one another in front of their close relatives and friends , often presided over by a religious figure such as a minister , priest , or rabbi , depending upon the faith of the couple . In traditional Christian ceremonies , the bride 's father will `` give away '' ( hand off ) the bride to the groom . Secular weddings are also common , often presided over by a judge , Justice of the Peace , or other municipal official . At a wedding reception , the father of the bride , in his role as host , regularly offers the first toast , thanking the guests for attending , offering tasteful remembrances of the bride 's childhood , and wishing the newlyweds a happy life together . The best man usually proposes a toast in the form of best wishes and congratulations to the newlyweds . A best man 's toast takes the form of a short speech ( 3 -- 5 minutes ) that combines a mixture of humor and sincerity . The actual `` toast '' is then delivered at the end of the speech and is a short phrase wishing the newlyweds a happy , healthy , loving life together . The maid of honor may follow suit , appropriately tailoring her comments to the bride . The groom may offer the final toast , thanking the bride 's parents for hosting the wedding , the wedding party for their participation , and finally dedicating the toast to the bridesmaids .

input.speaker: user
input.text: How are weddings celebrated?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: The typical wedding involves a couple proclaiming their commitment to one another in front of their close relatives and friends , often presided over by a religious figure such as a minister , priest , or rabbi , depending upon the faith of the couple . In traditional Christian ceremonies , the bride 's father will `` give away '' ( hand off ) the bride to the groom . Secular weddings are also common , often presided over by a judge , Justice of the Peace , or other municipal official . At a wedding reception , the father of the bride , in his role as host , regularly offers the first toast , thanking the guests for attending , offering tasteful remembrances of the bride 's childhood , and wishing the newlyweds a happy life together . The best man usually proposes a toast in the form of best wishes and congratulations to the newlyweds . A best man 's toast takes the form of a short speech ( 3 -- 5 minutes ) that combines a mixture of humor and sincerity . The actual `` toast '' is then delivered at the end of the speech and is a short phrase wishing the newlyweds a happy , healthy , loving life together . The maid of honor may follow suit , appropriately tailoring her comments to the bride . The groom may offer the final toast , thanking the bride 's parents for hosting the wedding , the wedding party for their participation , and finally dedicating the toast to the bridesmaids .

input.text: What is the meaning of the glass being broken at a Jewish wedding?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: The primary reason is that joy must always be tempered . input.text: The Defense of Marriage Act (DOMA) is a United States federal law that defines and protects the institution of marriage. enacted in 1996. Section 3 of DOMA defines marriage and spouse for federal purposes. Section 3 codified non-recognition of same - sex marriages for all federal purposes, including insurance benefits for government employees, social security survivors ' benefits, immigration, bankruptcy, and the filing of joint tax returns, as well as excluding same - sex spouses from the scope of laws protecting families of federal officers, laws evaluating financial aid eligibility, and federal ethics laws applicable to opposite - sex spouses.

input.text: Can you summarize the differences between good bots and bad bots?

input.speaker: user
input.text: Can you summarize the differences between good bots and bad bots?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: Bots are software programs that typically use a "headless browser" to make HTTP requests and other actions. A bot that performs a helpful service can be considered "good." For example, customer service chatbots, search engine crawlers, and performance monitoring bots are generally good bots. Good bots look for and abide by the rules outlined in a website's robots.txt file and read them and avoid that webpage. On the other hand, bad bots range from malicious to simply annoying; for instance, breaking into user accounts to steal data or buying concert tickets online to assist scalpers. Malicious bots typically don't  check a website's robots.txt file before accessing the website, which generates the need for bot management.

input.speaker: user
input.text: What are the features in the CIS bot?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: One of the features of CIS bot is JavaScript detections to help CIS classify traffic as automated or human. It works via ingesting a small amount of JavaScript into client devices using Google’s Picasso fingerprinting technique and factoring those Picasso results into bot scores. This detection technique gathers general data about the machines reaching CIS and cannot identify specific individuals. Additional steps are taken to anonymize and phase out data for privacy.

input.speaker: user
input.text: How does CIS manage bots?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: Cloudflare Bot Management, which is part of IBM Cloud® Internet Services (CIS), manages bots by collecting data from requests flowing through its network every day. With this data, powered by Cloudflare's machine learning and behavior analysis, CIS can identify likely bot activity and can provide you with information on how to allow or disallow specific bot traffic using Firewall Rules. The detection mechanisms used by CIS produce their own scores, which are then combined to form a single score.

input.speaker: user
input.text: How can I update machine leaning models?

input.speaker: agent
input.text:
You  can enable auto-updates on the machine learning models to receive newest models. To enable auto-updates:
1. Log in to the CIS and select your account and domain.
2. Go toSecurity > Bots.    
3. Select Configure Bot Management.  
4. Enable auto-updates to the machine learning model.  

Enabling auto-updates for the machine learning model upgrades you to the latest version immediately. You can toggle the button off within 24 hours to revert to the previous version. To make changes after 24 hours, contact support.

Malicious bots typically don't  check a website's robots.txt file before accessing the website, which generates the need for bot management.

input.speaker: user
input.text: What are the features in the CIS bot?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: One of the features of CIS bot is JavaScript detections to help CIS classify traffic as automated or human. It works via ingesting a small amount of JavaScript into client devices using Google’s Picasso fingerprinting technique and factoring those Picasso results into bot scores. This detection technique gathers general data about the machines reaching CIS and cannot identify specific individuals. Additional steps are taken to anonymize and phase out data for privacy.

input.speaker: user
input.text: How does CIS manage bots?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: Cloudflare Bot Management, which is part of IBM Cloud® Internet Services (CIS), manages bots by collecting data from requests flowing through its network every day. With this data, powered by Cloudflare's machine learning and behavior analysis, CIS can identify likely bot activity and can provide you with information on how to allow or disallow specific bot traffic using Firewall Rules. The detection mechanisms used by CIS produce their own scores, which are then combined to form a single score.

input.text: How can I update machine leaning models?

input.speaker: agent
input.text:
You  can enable auto-updates on the machine learning models to receive newest models. To enable auto-updates:
1. Log in to the CIS and select your account and domain.
2. Go toSecurity > Bots.    
3. Select Configure Bot Management.  
4. Enable auto-updates to the machine learning model.  

Enabling auto-updates for the machine learning model upgrades you to the latest version immediately. You can toggle the button off within 24 hours to revert to the previous version. To make changes after 24 hours, contact support.

input.speaker: user
input.text: By the way, what is a secret?

input.speaker: agent
input.text:
A secret is any piece of data that is sensitive within the context of an application or service.

Secrets include all of the following but are not limited to:
- Passwords of any type (database logins, OS accounts, functional IDs, and so on)
- API keys
- Long-lived authentication tokens (OAuth2, GitHub, IAM, and so on)
- SSH keys
- Encryption keys
- Other private keys (PKI/TLS certificates, HMAC keys, signing keys, and so on)
- Any type of credential used to access a confidential system

  It is critical that secrets are securely protected through their entire lifecycle.

input.text: It is important to note that trading stocks involves significant risk, and it is not recommended to invest money that you are not prepared to lose. Pump-and-dump scams are indeed very real, but the scale of a single scam isn't anywhere near the type of heist you see in movies like Trading Places. Stock market is like poker: you don't take the same risks when it is fake money and thus you don't learn the same lessons from your mistakes. Picking individual stocks and hoping for the value to go up in a relatively short time-frame is similar to gambling (rule #0: use only money that you don't need). Therefore, it is crucial to educate yourself and be smart when engaging in stock trading.

input.speaker: user
input.text: How much money do I need to invest in mutual funds or ETFs

input.speaker: agent
input.text: The minimum investment for mutual funds can vary depending on the fund, but it is often around $2000. However, $5000 isn't enough to give you much flexibility in achieving a balanced portfolio. For ETFs, you can invest in funds with a minimum amount subject to the fund's requirements. Keep in mind that buying and selling ETFs will cost you a commission, typically around 2-3% of your investment. When investing small amounts, you should consider the substantial toll that commissions will take on your investment. It is recommended to have at least $5000 to invest in stocks or ETFs to minimize the impact of commissions. If you have a smaller amount to invest, you might consider a Vanguard mutual fund, which will not charge commissions and will have annual expenses only slightly higher than the corresponding ETF.

Stock market is like poker: you don't take the same risks when it is fake money and thus you don't learn the same lessons from your mistakes. Picking individual stocks and hoping for the value to go up in a relatively short time-frame is similar to gambling (rule #0: use only money that you don't need). Therefore, it is crucial to educate yourself and be smart when engaging in stock trading.

input.speaker: user
input.text: Should I only invest in mutual funds?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: Choosing and investing in only one stock is risky. You can lower your risk by diversifying, or investing in lots of different stocks. That means investing in a mutual fund or Exchange Traded Funds (ETFs). Mutual funds, offer diversification, which can help spread risk. Index-based mutual funds invest in a broad range of securities including both stocks and bonds. I would recommend sticking to mutual funds and saving accounts for most investors.

input.speaker: user
input.text: What are ETFs?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: An ETF is an ""Exchange Traded Fund"". It trades like a stock, on the stock market. Basically by buying one ETF, you can have ownership in the underlying companies that make up the ETF. An ETF does not track any one individual stock. It is a marketable security that tracks an index, a commodity, bonds, or a basket of assets like an index fund. ETFs can be a cost-effective way to gain exposure to a broad portfolio of investments. Like mutual funds, ETFs can offer diversification, which can help spread risk. However, unlike mutual funds, ETFs have a higher liquidity and they can be traded at any time during the trading hours.

input.speaker: user
input.text: How much money do I need to invest in mutual funds or ETFs

input.speaker: agent
input.text: The minimum investment for mutual funds can vary depending on the fund, but it is often around $2000. However, $5000 isn't enough to give you much flexibility in achieving a balanced portfolio. For ETFs, you can invest in funds with a minimum amount subject to the fund's requirements. Keep in mind that buying and selling ETFs will cost you a commission, typically around 2-3% of your investment. When investing small amounts, you should consider the substantial toll that commissions will take on your investment. It is recommended to have at least $5000 to invest in stocks or ETFs to minimize the impact of commissions. If you have a smaller amount to invest, you might consider a Vanguard mutual fund, which will not charge commissions and will have annual expenses only slightly higher than the corresponding ETF.

input.speaker: user
input.text: How long is "long term investing"?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: Staying invested in stock market over the long term will almost always be profitable if you spread your investments across different companies or even the index but the key here is long term which is 10+ years. Long term data shows 40 years worth of investing put the investor far ahead. Even a retiree is still a long term investor as life spans of 90 are common. The concept of "long-term investing" can vary depending on the context and the individual investor's financial goals. Generally, 5 years is a reasonable time period to invest in a stock.

input.speaker: user
input.text: When do I have to start saving for my retirement?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: It is generally recommended to start as early as possible. Everyone thinks that they don't make enough to start saving. Don't put it off. Save some money out of each paycheck even if it is only $50. Now for the bad news. $1mm is not enough to retire on. The general rule of thumb is that you should have 25x your annual expenses saved when you retire. The only time to stop saving money for retirement is when you have enough money to retire tomorrow. Remember: you are likely to live to 100, so you better save enough to live that long. I don't trust social security to be there. 35 is not mid-life. You're on the tail end of the age to get started on retirement planning. There's a table on one of the FIRE (Financial Independent, Retire Early) websites that shows a graph between % of income saved and the number of years it takes to save enough to be financially independent.

input.speaker: user
input.text: How many film adaptations of Hamlet are there?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: I do not have specific information about the exact number of film adaptations of Hamlet. However, the earliest screen success recorded for Hamlet was Sara Bernhardt's 5-minute film of the fencing scene, which was produced in 1900. The film was an early attempt at combining sound and film , music and words were recorded on phonograph records , to be played along with the film . Seven silent versions were released from 1907-1921. Laurence Olivier adapted, produced, directed and starred in his screen version in 1948. Tony Richardson directed  the first Hamlet film in color in 1969. Franco A version by Franco Zeffirelli, with Mel Gibson and Glenn Close came out in 1990, and Kenneth Branagh's version was released in 1996. A version set in contemporary Manhattan and starring Ethan Hawke dates back to 2000. There have also been several films that transposed the general storyline of Hamlet or elements thereof to other settings . For example , the 2014 Bollywood film Haider is an adaptation set in Kashmir . There have also been many films which included performances of scenes from Hamlet as a play - within - a-film .

input.speaker: user
input.text: What about Romeo and Juliet?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: Just like Hamlet, Romeo and Juliet has been adapted multiple times for the small and large screens. It may be the most filmed play of all time, with notable theatrical releases such as George Cukor's 1936 production, Franco Zeffirelli's 1968 version, and Baz Luhrmann's 1996 MTV-inspired Romeo + Juliet. The play was first filmed in the silent era by Georges Méliès, although his film is now lost. The first sound recording of the play on film was in The Hollywood Revue of 1929, where John Gilbert recited the balcony scene opposite Norma Shearer. In 1960 , Peter Ustinov 's cold - war stage parody , Romanoff and Juliet was filmed. film A popular film of a musical theatre adaptation, West Side Story was made in 1961 . This version updated the setting to mid-20th - century New York City and the warring families to ethnic gangs. Other musical adaptations include Terrence Mann 's 1999 rock musical William Shakespeare 's Romeo and Juliet , co-written with Jerome Korman , Gérard Presgurvic 's 2001 Roméo et Juliette , de la Haine à l'Amour and Riccardo Cocciante 's 2007 Giulietta & Romeo . In 2013 , Sanjay Leela Bhansali directed the Bollywood film Goliyon Ki Raasleela Ram - Leela , a contemporary version of the play. which was a commercial and critical success.

input.text: Is Richard III historically accurate?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: Shakespeare's Richard III is a dramatic interpretation of history rather than a strict historical account. While the play is based on the true story of King Richard III of England, it contains numerous historical inaccuracies which can be attributed partly to Shakespeare 's sources, such as Holinshed 's Chronicles, and there is no doubt that Shakespeare drew heavily on Sir Thomas More 's account of Richard III as a criminal and tyrant as inspiration for his own rendering. Shakespeare and the Tudor chroniclers who influenced him, were obliged to portray the downfall of the Plantagenet House of York and the victory of the House of Tudor as good conquering evil. This is usually attributed to bias as a result of a new regime being in place.

input.text: Following an investment guru a good idea?

input.speaker: user
input.text: Following an investment guru a good idea?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: Following an investment guru can be a good idea if you can afford the time and are looking for more in-depth, fun investment tips. However, it's crucial to understand what you're investing in, including the company's products, and to buy stock in good companies at good prices, considering factors like price to earnings, price to revenue, and price to book. Therefore, while following an investment guru can provide valuable insights, it's essential to conduct your own research and make informed decisions based on your understanding and investment goals. Blindly following other investors, including big investors or gurus, is not recommended, as their strategies may differ from yours. Instead, consider finding someone with similar goals to help guide your investment decisions.

input.text: don't know if I go with IRA or 401K...

input.speaker: agent
input.text: A 401K (pre-tax or Roth) account or an IRA (Deductible or Roth) account is a retirement account. Traditional IRA and 401(k) accounts allow you to make pre-tax contributions, giving you an immediate tax deduction when you contribute. Roth IRA, Roth 401(k) are funded with after tax money.  Choosing between an IRA and a 401K depends on your personal circumstances and preferences. Here are some key points to consider: * A 401K is typically offered by your employer, while an IRA is an individual retirement account that you set up for yourself. * A 401K may have more limited investment options compared to an IRA, but it may also offer matching contributions from your employer, a free money to you.  * If you expect to be in a lower tax bracket in retirement than you are now, a traditional IRA or 401K may be a better choice, as it allows you to defer taxes until retirement. It's a good idea to start with the 401K offered by your company, especially if they have matching funds. Ultimately, the best choice for you will depend on your individual circumstances, so it's a good idea to consult with a financial advisor or do further research to determine the best option for you.

input.text: do i need to pay for garbage/recycling/organics service? what if I do not have enough money?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: Yes, service rates are based upon the size of your garbage cart. According to the Piedmont Municipal Code, every owner of any premises is required to ensure that arrangements are made to properly dispose of solid waste, recyclable material, and organic waste created, produced, or accumulated on the premises. There are ways to reduce the cost of garbage collection service. For example, you may be able to switch to a smaller garbage cart to save money; or you can switch to curbside service.

input.speaker: user
input.text: can you please clarify the service rate here?

input.speaker: agent
input.text:
The service rates for single-family residences in the City of Piedmont are based on the size of the garbage cart. The rates for curbside collection are as follows: $94.76 per month for a 20-gallon garbage cart, $100.41 per month for a 35-gallon garbage cart, $138.33 per month for a 65-gallon garbage cart, and $154.91 per month for a 95-gallon garbage cart. Each residential account includes unlimited recycling and organics carts. On-premises (backyard) collection rates are different and are limited to a maximum size of 35 gallons. Additional garbage carts are available for an additional cost.
For residents of Madison County, NY, there is no charge for residential yard waste drop-off if the items are up to 6 inches in diameter and no longer than 10 feet in length. Commercial yard waste, stumps, and oversized tree limbs are charged per ton and must go over the scales before unloading.

input.text: Yes, service rates are based upon the size of your garbage cart. According to the Piedmont Municipal Code, every owner of any premises is required to ensure that arrangements are made to properly dispose of solid waste, recyclable material, and organic waste created, produced, or accumulated on the premises. There are ways to reduce the cost of garbage collection service. For example, you may be able to switch to a smaller garbage cart to save money; or you can switch to curbside service.

input.speaker: user
input.text: can you please clarify the service rate here?

input.speaker: agent
input.text:
The service rates for single-family residences in the City of Piedmont are based on the size of the garbage cart. The rates for curbside collection are as follows: $94.76 per month for a 20-gallon garbage cart, $100.41 per month for a 35-gallon garbage cart, $138.33 per month for a 65-gallon garbage cart, and $154.91 per month for a 95-gallon garbage cart. Each residential account includes unlimited recycling and organics carts. On-premises (backyard) collection rates are different and are limited to a maximum size of 35 gallons. Additional garbage carts are available for an additional cost.
For residents of Madison County, NY, there is no charge for residential yard waste drop-off if the items are up to 6 inches in diameter and no longer than 10 feet in length. Commercial yard waste, stumps, and oversized tree limbs are charged per ton and must go over the scales before unloading.

During weeks when these holidays fall on a weekday, pickup is one day later for the rest of the week, including Saturday. LOJO Technology will also offer quarterly Saturday collection dates for those who cannot make it during the week. 2024 Saturday dates from 9 AM to 1 PM for electronic recycling. To set up an HHW appointment for Hazardous waste, please fill out the reservation form and enter your preferred date/time. Miller Environmental Group, Inc. is open Monday - Friday, 8 AM to 3 PM.

input.text: what is electronic waste and hazardous waste here?

input.text: Electronic waste (e-waste) considers items such as TVs, computer accessories, cables, small accessories, inkjet printers/scanners/fax machines, laser printers, desktop copiers, stereos, audio/video equipment, video games, laptops, cellular/cordless phones and answering machines, power supplies. Hazardous waste, on the other hand, refers to any household material that is toxic or corrosive, or can catch fire, react or explode under certain conditions. Examples include bleaches, cleaning solvents, pesticides, pool chemicals and more. In Madison County, NY, there is a free household hazardous waste program for its residents where they can bring their waste to Miller Environmental Group in Syracuse, NY. Appointments are required and there is a limit of 1 visit per household per year.

input.text: I am allergic to latex...

input.speaker: agent
input.text: If you are allergic to latex, you should avoid contact with natural rubber latex products. If you have to wear gloves in your work, ask your employer for nonlatex gloves. Avoid areas where you might inhale the powder from latex gloves worn by other workers. Wear a personal medical emergency ID bracelet. If prescribed by your physician, carry an emergency epinephrine kit in case you are accidentally exposed to latex and go into anaphylactic shock. Learn how to use it and make sure family members, friends and fellow employees know how to use it. If you use latex-based products on the job, alert your employer about your allergy. Before dental and medical procedures or examinations, alert health care providers about your allergy. Ask to be scheduled as the first patient in the morning to minimize your exposure to airborne latex particles. Participate in educational and training programs regarding latex allergy. If the allergy sufferer is a child, alert school and/or day care providers. Work with your allergist, local hospital and medical facilities to find latex-safe environments where you can go for treatment. Contact your local emergency responders (police, fire and ambulance service) to inform them of your condition so a plan of care can be developed before there is an actual emergency. Health care facilities are beginning to create latex-safe treatment areas and surgical suites to accommodate people who are allergic to latex. Check with your local hospital.

Only home-owners who do not pay to have debris removed but personally transport the debris to the solid waste and/or recycling facility are exempt from the exclusive franchise agreement.

input.text: How do I file a complaint with the state?

input.speaker: user
input.text: How do I file a complaint with the state?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: To file a complaint with the New York State Attorney General, you can visit their official website and select the category that best fits your purpose. For consumer issues, you can choose from categories like goods or services purchase, credit cards, credit reports, and personal finance, social media, personal data and information privacy, vehicle lease or purchase, and lemon law. If you cannot complete the form online or need additional assistance, you can call their OAG Help Line at 1-800-771-7755. Please note that patterns of complaints often help in allocating law enforcement resources, and filing a false complaint is punishable as a Class A Misdemeanor.

input.text:
Letitia James is the 67th Attorney General for the state of New York, and she is the first woman of color to hold statewide office in New York and the first woman to be elected Attorney General. 

Some recent highlights: Attorney General James Secures $740,000 from Online Mental Health Provider for its Burdensome Cancellation Process; she reaches $310,000 Settlement-in-Principle with Syracuse Landlord for Failing to Address Lead-Based Paint Hazards; she secures $15.2 Million Judgment Against Unlicensed Cannabis Store Owner in Upstate New York; and she also wins Landmark Victory in Case Against Donald Trump.

input.speaker: user
input.text: How do I file a complaint with the state?

input.speaker: agent
input.text: To file a complaint with the New York State Attorney General, you can visit their official website and select the category that best fits your purpose. For consumer issues, you can choose from categories like goods or services purchase, credit cards, credit reports, and personal finance, social media, personal data and information privacy, vehicle lease or purchase, and lemon law. If you cannot complete the form online or need additional assistance, you can call their OAG Help Line at 1-800-771-7755. Please note that patterns of complaints often help in allocating law enforcement resources, and filing a false complaint is punishable as a Class A Misdemeanor.

In contrast, the California Department of Consumer Affairs' Consumer Service Representatives are available to answer consumer, licensee, and applicant questions in over 200 different languages by phone from 8:00 a.m. to 5:00 p.m., Monday through Friday, except on the first and third Wednesday of each month, when they open at 8:30 a.m., and on state-observed holidays.
